During a Cabinet of Ministers meeting on 24 February, Tymoshenko stated, "The moment of truth has arrived: The decision whether or not to side with Yanukovych will show who values the preservation of Ukraine's independence and self-identity and who does not."[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/60498/ Tymoshenko fights on, refuses to recognize Yanukovych win], ''Kyiv Post'' (25 February 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614035540/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/60498/ |date=14 June 2012}} Tymoshenko and her party, Bloc Yulia Tymoshenko, boycotted the inauguration ceremony of Yanukovych on 25 February 2010.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60438/ Half-empty chamber greets Ukraine's new president], ''Kyiv Post'' (25 February 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120612031635/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60438/ |date=12 June 2012}} [[File:Flickr - europeanpeoplesparty - EPP Summit September 2010 (12).jpg|thumb|Tymoshenko in September 2010]] If the Second Tymoshenko Government could not be preserved, Tymoshenko stated on 22 February 2010, she would go into Parliamentary opposition.[https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/ea5wzazv Yulia Tymoshenko's address to the people of Ukraine], Yulia Tymoshenko official website (22 February 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150723020650/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/ea5wzazv |date=23 July 2015}} On 3 March 2010, the Ukrainian Parliament passed a motion of no confidence in the second Tymoshenko Government in which the cabinet was dismissed with 243 lawmakers voting in favour out of 450{{cite news |url= https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/60896/ |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100311220422/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/60896/ |archive-date=11 March 2010 |title=Ukraine parliament votes out Tymoshenko's government |publisher=Kyiv Post|date=3 March 2010}} (including seven lawmakers of Bloc Yulia Tymoshenko[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-11381367 MPs desert defeated Ukraine candidate Yulia Tymoshenko] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201225149/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-11381367 |date=1 December 2017 }}, BBC News (21 September 2010)). (Prime Minister Tymoshenko had demanded this vote herself on 1 March 2010.)[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60682/ Tymoshenko demands parliament should consider her government's dismissal on 2 March], ''Kyiv Post'' (1 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614034804/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60682/ |date=14 June 2012}} On 2 March 2010, the coalition had already lost the parliamentary majority. Before the vote on 3 March, Tymoshenko again stated, "If the dismissal of the government is passed today, at that very same moment our government will leave the cabinet. Our political force will cross into the opposition."[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60890/ Tymoshenko says cabinet won't stay on as caretaker], ''Kyiv Post'' (3 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110124140417/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60890/ |date=24 January 2011}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60892/ Tymoshenko: Government members will immediately leave offices after Rada's decision on cabinet dismissal], ''Kyiv Post'' (3 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100306073012/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60892/ |date=6 March 2010}} Tymoshenko blamed the Lytvyn Bloc and "Our Ukraine, including the leader of Our Ukraine, who announced the position of the faction" for the fall of the cabinet.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60812/ Tymoshenko: Dissolution of parliamentary coalition illegal], ''Kyiv Post'' (2 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614035722/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60812/ |date=14 June 2012}} Tymoshenko resigned from the prime minister post on 4 March 2010.{{cite news |url= https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60998/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100307140238/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60998/ |archive-date=7 March 2010 |title=Press secretary: Tymoshenko vacates premier's post |publisher=Kyiv Post|date=4 March 2010}} Fellow BYuT member [[Oleksandr Turchynov]] was empowered to fulfill the prime minister's duties until a new government was formed on 4 March 2010.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60963/ Cabinet: Turchynov will fulfill premier's duties until new government is formed], ''Kyiv Post'' (4 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614035855/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/60963/ |date=14 June 2012}} On 9[https://web.archive.org/web/20100311014743/https://en.rian.ru/exsoviet/20100309/158136299.html Ex-premier Tymoshenko calls for Ukrainians to join opposition], [[RIA Novosti]] (9 March 2010) and 15 March[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/61738/ Tymoshenko calls on opposition to unite], ''Kyiv Post'' (15 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605061908/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/61738/ |date=5 June 2011}} 2010, Tymoshenko called on "all of the national patriotic forces" to unite against Yanukovych.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/61562/ Yushchenko jumps back into political fray, visiting relatively friendly territory in Lviv], ''Kyiv Post'' (11 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100315012731/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/61562/ |date=15 March 2010}} On 16 March, a [[First Azarov government|shadow government including BYuT]] was established.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/61879/ Eight parties sign agreement on creation of united opposition], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 March 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100322191950/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/61879/ |date=22 March 2010}} On 10 May 2010, the [[People's Committee to Protect Ukraine]] was established, of which Tymoshenko is one of the representatives.{{Cite web |title=Ющенко і Яценюк проігнорували Комітет захисту України |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2010/05/10/5025903/ |access-date=2023-07-08 |website=Ukrainska Pravda |language=uk |archive-date=4 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181104170116/https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2010/05/10/5025903/ |url-status=live }}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66342/ Police move to dampen protest turnout], ''Kyiv Post'' (11 May 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110606033056/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66342/ |date=6 June 2011}} Tymoshenko was against the [[2010 Ukrainian-Russian Naval Base for Natural Gas treaty]], as she believes the agreement harms Ukraine's national interests.[https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/u262owsn Yulia Tymoshenko: Yanukovych-Medvedev agreement will be terminated], Official website of Yulia Tymoshenko (23 April 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140806045513/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/u262owsn |date=6 August 2014}} On 12 May 2010, Ukraine's prosecutor's office illegally re-opened a 2004 [[criminal case]], which had been closed by the Supreme Court of Ukraine in January 2005 against Tymoshenko regarding accusations that she had tried to bribe Supreme Court judges. As she left the prosecutor's office on 12 May, Tymoshenko told journalists she had been summoned to see investigators again on 17 May, and she linked the move to Russian President Medvedev's visit to Ukraine on 17 and 18 May 2010.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66478/ Old bribe case re-opened against Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (12 May 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110606041156/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66478/ |date=6 June 2011}}[https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/in_pictures/8677322.stm Day in pictures (12 May 2010)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220301144507/http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/in_pictures/8677322.stm |date=1 March 2022 }}, BBC News (12 May 2010) Tymoshenko also claimed that she was told by "all the offices of the Prosecutor General's Office" that President Yanukovych had personally instructed the Prosecutor General's Office to find any grounds to prosecute her.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66449/ Tymoshenko claims president ordered prosecutors to put her in jail], ''Kyiv Post'' (12 May 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110606040509/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66449/ |date=6 June 2011}} In a press conference on 12 May, Yanukovych's representative in the Parliament, Yury Miroshnychenko, dismissed Tymoshenko's statement about Yanukovych's personal interest in prosecuting her. "Yanukovych is against political repression for criticism of the regime," Miroshnychenko stated.{{cite web|url=https://zik.com.ua/en/news/2010/05/12/228377|title=Yanukovych bears no grudges against Tymoshenko|work=zik.com.ua|date=12 May 2010|access-date=7 November 2015|archive-date=18 October 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151018204136/http://zik.com.ua/en/news/2010/05/12/228377|url-status=live}} === The most famous criminal cases against Tymoshenko === [[File:Flickr - europeanpeoplesparty - EPP Summit September 2010 (111).jpg|thumb|Tymoshenko and [[Mikheil Saakashvili]], September 2010]] On 15 December 2010, the General Prosecutor's Office instituted a criminal case against Tymoshenko, alleging that she misused funds received by Ukraine within the framework of the [[Kyoto Protocol]]. She was officially charged on 20 December 2010.{{cite news|url=https://www.unian.info/politics/437990-general-prosecutors-office-instituted-criminal-case-against-tymoshenko.html|title=General Prosecutor's Office instituted criminal case against Tymoshenko|work=UNIAN|date=15 December 2010|access-date=7 November 2015|archive-date=16 April 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190416204755/https://www.unian.info/politics/437990-general-prosecutors-office-instituted-criminal-case-against-tymoshenko.html|url-status=live}}[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/56532.html Tymoshenko says she is charged with misuse of 'green money'] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010104640/http://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/56532.html |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (20 December 2010)[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/56556.html Former Ukrainian prime minister charged with abuse of power – document] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010104623/http://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/56556.html |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (21 December 2010) Tymoshenko denied the money had been spent on pensions, insisting it was still at the disposal of the environment ministry. She called the investigation against her a [[witch-hunt]].[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-12042561 Ukraine ex-PM Tymoshenko charged with misusing funds] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201225215/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-12042561 |date=1 December 2017 }}, BBC News (20 December 2010) According to government officials, the criminal case against Tymoshenko was a legitimate attempt to uncover corruption by the previous administration. In the case of "Kyoto money" American experts "Covington & Burling" and "BDO USA" stated the following: "the Documents that we were able to see, clearly point out that the Kyoto Target balance account in the amount of approximately 3.3 billion on the date of receipt remained unchanged throughout the period that is considered, and that, moreover, Kyoto funds have not been touched at all in 2009. Since the balance of this account remained unchanged on the date of receipt, any accusations that Prime Minister Tymoshenko "used" these funds contrary to their intended purpose, are obviously false".[https://www.pravda.com.ua/articles/2011/06/18/6308683/ Joint statement Covington & Burling LLP and BDO regarding the accusations against Tymoshenko June 18, 2011] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010155836/http://www.pravda.com.ua/articles/2011/06/18/6308683/ |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[Pravda]] (18 June 2011) On 7 August 2014, the Chairman of the State Treasury service Tatiana Slyuz confirmed that the Tymoshenko government has never spent "Kyoto money", the funds were on special accounts and in 2010 were transferred to the Yanukovych government.[https://www.rbc.ua/ukr/news/pravitelstvo-timoshenko-ne-tratilo-kiotskie-sredstva--07082014151000 Tymoshenko's government did not spend Kyoto money", the state Treasury] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010210641/https://www.rbc.ua/ukr/news/pravitelstvo-timoshenko-ne-tratilo-kiotskie-sredstva--07082014151000 |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[RBC Information Systems|Rbk]] (7 August 2014) Tymoshenko was not arrested, but ordered not to leave Kyiv while the inquiry was under way.{{cite news|last=Levy|first=Clifford J.|title=Ukraine: Investigation of Ex-Premier|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2010/12/16/world/europe/16briefs-Ukraine.html?_r=1&src=twrhp|access-date=29 July 2012|date=15 December 2010|newspaper=The New York Times|archive-date=4 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181104165951/https://www.nytimes.com/2010/12/16/world/europe/16briefs-Ukraine.html?_r=1&src=twrhp|url-status=live}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/92952/ Criminal proceedings keep Tymoshenko from European People's Party summit], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 December 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110220161004/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/92952/ |date=20 February 2011}} In the same case, the environment minister in the second Tymoshenko Government, Georgiy Filipchuk, was detained. Filipchuk was the third minister from this government to face criminal charges since its fall in March 2010 (prosecutors charged former Interior Minister Yuriy Lutsenko with abuse of office in early December 2010, and former economy minister [[Bohdan Danylyshyn Mykhailovych|Bohdan Danylyshyn]] was detained in the [[Czech Republic]] in October 2010 on similar charges).[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/93022/ Prosecutors launch probe of Tymoshenko, arrest her environment minister], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 December 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120312214744/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/93022/ |date=12 March 2012}} Lawmakers of BYuT blocked the rostrum and presidium of the Verkhovna Rada the next day in protest against this.[https://www.unian.info/politics/438290-byut-lawmakers-blocked-rostrum-and-presidium-of-vru.html BYUT lawmakers blocked rostrum and presidium of VRU] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010212149/https://www.unian.info/politics/438290-byut-lawmakers-blocked-rostrum-and-presidium-of-vru.html |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[UNIAN]] (16 December 2010) That same day, the [[European People's Party]] issued a statement in which it "condemns the growth of aggressive, politically motivated pressure by the Ukrainian authorities on the opposition and its leader Yulia Tymoshenko."[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/92992/ European People's Party condemns 'politically motivated' government pressure on Ukrainian opposition], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 December 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111129213456/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/92992/ |date=29 November 2011}} Tymoshenko dismissed the probe as "terror against the opposition by President Yanukovych."[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/92935/ Tymoshenko probed for abuse of office], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 December 2010) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111129213303/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/92935/ |date=29 November 2011}} Earlier that month, Ukraine's Prosecutor General [[Viktor Pshonka]] had stated that there were no political reasons for the interrogations of the opposition leaders Tymoshenko, Lutsenko and Oleksandr Turchynov.{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/92576/|title=Prosecutor general says there were no politics in questioning Tymoshenko and Turchynov|work=Kyiv Post|date=10 December 2010|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110127114813/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/92576/|archive-date=27 January 2011}} New corruption charges against Tymoshenko were filed on 27 January 2011.{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/95806/|title=New charges against Ukraine's ex-premier|work=Kyiv Post|date=27 January 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110303153346/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/95806|archive-date=3 March 2011}}{{cite news|url=https://www.unian.info/politics/453536-tymoshenko-authorities-have-fabricated-another-charge-against-me.html|title=Tymoshenko: authorities have fabricated another charge against me|work=[[UNIAN]]|date=28 January 2011|access-date=29 January 2016|archive-date=10 October 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010212059/https://www.unian.info/politics/453536-tymoshenko-authorities-have-fabricated-another-charge-against-me.html|url-status=live}} She was accused of using 1,000 medical vehicles for campaigning in the presidential elections of 2010. According to Tymoshenko, the charges were false and part of "Yanukovych's campaign to silence the opposition." Subsequently, in 2010, the results of the audit of the accounts chamber revealed that the acquisition of these vehicles was provided for from 2008 in the article 87 of the Budget code, the State budget-2009 and article 13 of the Law of Ukraine "On state target programs". In June 2011, the case on the "Kyoto money" and of medical assistance vehicles to the village were suspended — after auditing company "BDO USA", which has branches in over one hundred countries around the world, and a large law firm "Covington & Burling" investigated these cases and stated that they "are not worth the paper on which they are written."[https://zakon2.rada.gov.ua/laws/show/1621-15 the law of Ukraine On state targeted programmes] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190928180625/https://zakon2.rada.gov.ua/laws/show/1621-15 |date=28 September 2019 }}, ''Zakon2.rada.gov.ua'' (12 December 2012){{Cite web|url=https://zakon.rada.gov.ua/go/835-17|title=Про Державний бюджет України на 2009 рік|website=Офіційний вебпортал парламенту України}} A third criminal case against Tymoshenko in connection with alleged [[abuse of power]] during the 2009 Russia–Ukraine gas dispute was opened on 10 April 2011.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/102100/ Ukraine investigates Tymoshenko over Russia gas deal (updated)], ''Kyiv Post'' (11 April 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120615000902/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/102100/ |date=15 June 2012}}[https://www.upi.com/Science_News/Resource-Wars/2011/04/12/Gas-charges-absurd-says-Tymoshenko/UPI-76601302618333/ Gas charges 'absurd', says Tymoshenko] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171010140509/https://www.upi.com/Science_News/Resource-Wars/2011/04/12/Gas-charges-absurd-says-Tymoshenko/UPI-76601302618333/ |date=10 October 2017 }}, [[United Press International]] (12 April 2011) This case was labelled "absurd" by Tymoshenko. On 24 May 2011, prosecutors charged her in connection with this (third criminal) case.[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-13520048 Ukraine ex-premier Tymoshenko charged over gas deals] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180125/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-13520048 |date=9 January 2016 }}, BBC News (24 May 2011) She was not arrested.[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/69426.html Investigation into Tymoshenko's case completed, no need to arrest her, says PGO] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303201559/http://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/69426.html |date=3 March 2016 }}, [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (24 May 2011) On 26 April 2011, Tymoshenko sued businessman [[Dmytro Firtash]] and [[Switzerland|Swiss]]-based [[RosUkrEnergo]] in a [[US District Court]] in [[Manhattan]], accusing them of "defrauding Ukraine's citizenry by manipulating an arbitration court ruling" and "undermining the rule of law in Ukraine" in connection with a 2010 international arbitration court ruling in [[Stockholm]] that ordered Ukraine's state energy company [[Naftogaz]] to pay RosUkrEnergo 11 billion cubic meters (bcm) of natural gas to compensate for fuel it had "expropriated" plus 1.1 billion bcm as a penalty.[https://www.reuters.com/article/ukraine-naftogaz-lawsuit-idUSN276396520110427 Ukrainian ex-PM sues gas company, businessman in US court] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170914164719/http://www.reuters.com/article/ukraine-naftogaz-lawsuit-idUSN276396520110427 |date=14 September 2017 }}, [[Reuters]] (27 April 2011)[https://en.novayagazeta.ru/politics/48959.html Prisoner of independence] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111018073143/https://en.novayagazeta.ru/politics/48959.html |date=18 October 2011}}, [[Novaya Gazeta]] (12 October 2011) Throughout [[Viktor Yanukovych#Presidency (2010–2014)|Yanukovych's presidency]], Tymoshenko stayed very critical of his and the Azarov Government's performances and intentions which, among others, she accused of selling out to Russia and of being a "funeral of democracy."[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/8073640/Yulia-Tymoshenko-urges-West-to-stop-ignoring-Russian-takeover-of-Ukraine.html Yulia Tymoshenko urges West to stop ignoring Russian takeover of Ukraine] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180125/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/8073640/Yulia-Tymoshenko-urges-West-to-stop-ignoring-Russian-takeover-of-Ukraine.html |date=9 January 2016 }}, [[The Daily Telegraph]] (19 October 2010)[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-10817344 New media freedom battle grips Ukraine] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180125/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-10817344 |date=9 January 2016 }}, BBC News (30 July 2010)[http://www.cnn.com/2010/WORLD/europe/04/24/russia.ukraine/index.html?_s=PM:WORLD Thousands protest Russia-Ukraine deal] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131216012227/http://www.cnn.com/2010/WORLD/europe/04/24/russia.ukraine/index.html?_s=PM:WORLD |date=16 December 2013 }}, [[CNN]] (24 April 2010)[https://edition.cnn.com/2010/WORLD/europe/12/15/ukraine.corruption/index.html?iref=allsearch Ukraine's Tymoshenko questioned over corruption], [[CNN]] (15 December 2010) Tymoshenko has accused "many of Ukraine's neighbours" of turning a blind eye to "Yanukovych's strangulation of Ukraine's democracy, some openly celebrate the supposed 'stability' that his regime has imposed."{{cite news|first=Yulia|last=Tymoshenko|url=https://www.themoscowtimes.com/opinion/article/the-orange-revolution-betrayed/430471.html|title=The Orange Revolution Betrayed|publisher=[[The Moscow Times]]|date=7 February 2011|access-date=6 November 2015|archive-date=3 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303180557/http://www.themoscowtimes.com/opinion/article/the-orange-revolution-betrayed/430471.html|url-status=dead}} She believes "Ukraine can return to a democratic path of development only with an active [[civil society]] and support from the [[international community]]."[https://www.unian.info/politics/457926-tymoshenko-active-civil-society-will-help-return-democracy-to-ukraine.html Tymoshenko: active civil society will help return democracy to Ukraine], [[UNIAN]] (8 February 2011) ===2011 trial and imprisonment and other criminal cases against Tymoshenko=== {{main|Criminal cases against Yulia Tymoshenko since 2010}} {{See also|Criminal cases against supporters of Yulia Tymoshenko}} [[File:EPP Summit March 2011 (66) (cropped).jpg|thumb|Tymoshenko and [[German Prime Minister|Chancellor]] [[Angela Merkel]] at a March 2011 [[European People's Party]] summit in [[Brussels]]; the [[General Prosecutor of Ukraine]]'s Office lifted the travel ban imposed on Tymoshenko after [[U.S. Senator]] [[John McCain]] and European People's Party President [[Wilfried Martens]] officially invited her to this event.[https://www.mccain.senate.gov/public/index.cfm/press-releases?ID=91131d3c-dd4d-7612-e98c-d3f460e5c03e Statement by Senator John McCain & EPP President Wilfried Martens on Ukraine], [[European People's Party]] (7 March 2011) [https://en.for-ua.com/news/2011/03/17/161804.html Tymoshenko to present a picture of Ukrainian events in Brussels] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120329214313/https://en.for-ua.com/news/2011/03/17/161804.html |date=29 March 2012}}, for-ua.com (17 March 2011) ]] In May 2010, the Ukrainian General Prosecutor's office started a number of criminal cases against Tymoshenko which prevented her from normal political activity and from international travel to her allies in the West. The European Parliament passed a resolution condemning the Yanukovych government for persecution of Tymoshenko as well as for prosecution in the "Gas case" and other cases against her and her ministers.[https://www.europarl.europa.eu/document/activities/cont/201106/20110620ATT21953/20110620ATT21953EN.pdf European Parliament resolution of 9 June 2011 on Ukraine: the cases of Yulia Tymoshenko and other members of the former government], europarl.europa.eu (9 June 2011) On 24 June 2011, a trial started in the "gas case", concerning a contract signed in 2009 with Gazprom to supply natural gas to Ukraine. Tymoshenko was charged with abuse of power and [[embezzlement]], as the court found the deal anti-economic for the country and abusive.{{cite web|url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/photo-video/2011/06/24/6326224/|title=The trial of Tymoshenko. Started|publisher= Pravda.com.ua|date=24 June 2011|access-date=24 June 2011}} Tymoshenko's trial (she was charged in May 2011) for [[abuse of office]] concerning a natural gas imports contract signed with Russia in January 2009 started on 24 June 2011 in Kyiv.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/114375/ Boiko: Naftogaz will not withdraw civil suit against Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (7 October 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111015040651/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/114375/ |date=15 October 2011}}{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-14442659 | work=BBC News |date=8 August 2011 |title=Protesters gather as Tymoshenko trial resumes in Kiev |access-date=20 August 2011}} A number of criminal cases were also opened against former officials from the second Tymoshenko Government.{{cite news|first1=Peter|last1=Byrne|first2=Kateryna|last2=Grushenko|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/99516/|title=Jailhouse Watch: Many former top officials remain in jail for months|publisher=Kyiv Post|date=11 March 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111011144955/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/99516/|archive-date=11 October 2011}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/111586/ Yanukovych: Over 400 officials of current government stand trial] ''Kyiv Post'' (25 August 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110829115230/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/111586/ |date=29 August 2011}}{{#tag:ref|These criminal cases were opened against officials from the [[second Tymoshenko Government]] (prosecutors have not accused them of corruption, or assignment of funds, but rather have accused them of abuse of power): # Prime minister – Tymoshenko # Minister of Police – [[Yuriy Lutsenko]] # Minister of Defence – [[Valeriy Ivashchenko]] # Minister of Finance – {{ill|Danylyshyn|uk|Данилишин Богдан Михайлович|vertical-align=sup}} # Minister of Natural Resources – {{ill|Filipchuk|uk|Філіпчук Георгій Георгійович|vertical-align=sup}} # Deputy Minister of Justice – {{ill|Korneichuk|uk|Корнійчук Євген Володимирович|vertical-align=sup}} # Head of Customs of Ukraine – {{ill|Makarenko|uk|Макаренко Анатолій Вікторович|vertical-align=sup}} # Head of the regional customs – Shepitko # Head of the State Treasury of Ukraine – Slyuz; Deputy head – Gritsoun # Deputy head of "Naftogaz" (state monopoly on trade in gas and oil) – {{ill|Didenko|uk|Діденко Ігор Миколайович|vertical-align=sup}} # Governor of [[Dnipropetrovsk region]] (former Minister of Transport) – [[Viktor Bondar|Bondar]] # Minister and former mayor of [[Lviv]] – [[Vasyl Kuybida]]. Source: [https://file.liga.net/person/423-vasilii-kyibida.html Minister and former mayor of Lviv – Kuybida] # First Deputy Prime Minister [[Oleksandr Turchynov]] – repeatedly summoned for questioning|group=nb}} According to Yanukovych, those cases were indiscriminately made to fight [[corruption in Ukraine]].[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/112862/ Yanukovych vows to put an end to corruption] ''Kyiv Post'' (15 September 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120527010220/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/112862/ |date=27 May 2012}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/96507/ Yanukovych: 'Some misinform international community about Ukraine'] ''Kyiv Post'' (7 February 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110314144736/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/96507/ |date=14 March 2011}} Former President Yushchenko testified against Tymoshenko during the trial, which he called "a normal judicial process."[https://www.spiegel.de/international/europe/ultimate-betrayal-ukraine-retreats-to-a-dark-past-a-792458-2.html Ukraine Retreats to a Dark Past], [[Der Spiegel]] (18 October 2011) The trial against Tymoshenko has been referred to as "selective justice" and "political persecution" in statements by the [[United States|U.S.]], Russia, United Kingdom, Germany, Italy, Spain and other European countries; in statements by the [[European Union]], [[NATO]], the European People's Party; and in statements by human rights organizations such as [[Transparency International]], [[Freedom House]], [[Amnesty International]] and [[Human Rights Watch]].{{cite web |url=https://www.infosud.ru/international_news/20111011/256228918.html |title=Amnesty International believes that "the verdict Tymoshenko" is politically motivated |publisher=Infosud.ru |date=11 October 2011 |access-date=28 December 2013 |archive-date=28 October 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141028012742/https://www.infosud.ru/international_news/20111011/256228918.html |url-status=dead}} Following her conviction, Tymoshenko remained under [[criminal investigation]] for ten [[criminal acts]];[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/116273/ Piling cases on Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (3 November 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120429232839/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/116273/ |date=29 April 2012}} Ukrainian prosecutors have claimed Tymoshenko committed even more criminal acts.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/116274 Getting Yulia], ''Kyiv Post'' (3 November 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120430034116/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/116274 |date=30 April 2012}} Early in July 2011, the [[Ukrainian security service]] (SBU) opened a new criminal investigation into alleged non-delivery of goods by United Energy Systems of Ukraine (in 1996) to Russia for $405.5 million, the SBU maintains that Russia may claim this sum to the State budget of Ukraine (this criminal case was closed in Russia in December 2005 due to lapse of time). On 11 October 2011, the court found Tymoshenko guilty of abuse of power and sentenced her to seven years in prison, banned her from seeking elected office for her period of imprisonment, and ordered her to pay the state $188 million.[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-15250742 Ukraine ex-PM Yulia Tymoshenko jailed over gas deal], BBC News (11 October 2011)[https://zib.com.ua/ua/print/5728-the_tymoshenko_verdict_full_text_of_the_sentence.html The Tymoshenko verdict. Full text of the sentence], "Law & Business" (13 October 2011) She was convicted for exceeding her powers as [[Ukrainian Prime Minister|prime minister]] by ordering Naftogaz to sign the gas deal with Russia in 2009.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/114528/ Tymoshenko convicted, sentenced to seven years in prison, ordered to pay state $188 million (update)], ''Kyiv Post'' (11 October 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614062511/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/114528/ |date=14 June 2012}} Tymoshenko did appeal the sentence, which she compared to [[Stalin's Great Terror]], on 24 October 2011.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/115592/ Tymoshenko files appeal against court ruling in gas case], ''Kyiv Post'' (24 October 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111224232310/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/115592/ |date=24 December 2011}} [[#Deputy Prime Minister for fuel and energy|A 2001 criminal case on state funds embezzlement and tax evasion charges against Tymoshenko]] was reopened in Ukraine on 24 October 2011.{{citation needed|date=November 2020}} On 4 November 2011, the [[Law of Ukraine#Types of corporations|Ukrainian tax police]] resumed four criminal cases against Tymoshenko.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/116410/ Tax police resumes four criminal cases against Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (7 November 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111221145738/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/116410/ |date=21 December 2011}} She was charged for these cases on 10 November 2011.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/116741/ Ukraine heaps more charges on Tymoshenko (updated)], ''Kyiv Post'' (11 November 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120614062548/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/116741/ |date=14 June 2012}}[https://www.unian.info/politics/567401-vlasenko-investigators-illegally-presented-yulia-tymoshenko-with-new-charges.html Vlasenko: investigators illegally presented Yulia Tymoshenko with new charges], [[UNIAN]] (11 November 2011)[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/85309.html Ukraine tax authorities file charges against Tymoshenko], [[Interfax Ukraine]] (11 November 2011) Tymoshenko was re-arrested (while in prison) on 8 December 2011, after a Ukrainian court ordered her [[indefinite detention without trial|indefinite arrest]] as part of the investigation of alleged tax evasion and theft of government funds (between 1996 and 2000) by United Energy Systems of Ukraine. Again, the EU showed concern over this.[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118550/ Ukraine 're-arrests' Tymoshenko, EU sends envoy], ''Kyiv Post'' (9 December 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120501022721/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118550/ |date=1 May 2012}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/118529/ Teixeira: EU concerned about new arrest Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (9 December 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120428200432/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/118529/ |date=28 April 2012}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118531/ Tymoshenko faces indefinite arrest], ''Kyiv Post'' (9 December 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111216210350/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118531/ |date=16 December 2011}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118522/ Lawyer: Tymoshenko arrested 'for life'], ''Kyiv Post'' (9 December 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120502052255/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/118522/ |date=2 May 2012}}[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-16156740 Jailed Tymoshenko seen by EU Commissioner Stefan Fuele], [[BBC News]] (13 December 2011) On 23 December 2011, Tymoshenko lost her appeal against her sentence for abuse of power.[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-16317361 Ukraine's Tymoshenko loses appeal against jail term], [[BBC News]] (23 December 2011)[https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119545 Tymoshenko sentence in gas supply case takes legal force], ''Kyiv Post'' (23 December 2011) {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111230103056/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119545 |date=30 December 2011}} She and her lawyers had boycotted the appeal proceedings, claiming that the "[[Judiciary of Ukraine|judicial system]] and [[Justice in Ukraine|justice]] are totally non-existent in Ukraine today."[https://web.archive.org/web/20120120170922/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119454/ Official statement by Yulia Tymoshenko], ''Kyiv Post'' (22 December 2011) Tymoshenko lodged a complaint against the verdict at the [[European Court of Human Rights]], which was given priority treatment by the court.[https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/app/conversion/pdf/?library=ECHR&id=003-3787303-4335244&filename=Communicated%20case%20Tymoshenko%20v.%20Ukraine%2019.12.11.pdf Press release], [[ECtHR]] (20 December 2011) On 30 December 2011, Tymoshenko was transferred to the [[Kachanivska penal colony]] in Kharkiv.[https://web.archive.org/web/20120107153311/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119832/ Tymoshenko moved to prison in Kharkiv (updated)], ''Kyiv Post'' (30 December 2011)[https://web.archive.org/web/20120105034240/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119824/ Tymoshenko moved to Kachanivska penal colony in Kharkiv (updated)], ''Kyiv Post'' (30 December 2011) In early January 2012, Tymoshenko's husband Oleksandr was granted [[Right of asylum|asylum]] in the Czech Republic, which he had requested at the end of the previous year.[https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180138/http://uk.reuters.com/article/uk-ukraine-tymoshenko-czech-idUKTRE8050Q520120106?feedType=RSS&feedName=worldNews Czechs give asylum to spouse of Ukraine's Tymoshenko] [[Reuters]] (6 January 2012){{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-16439321|title=Husband of Ukraine's Tymoshenko wins Czech asylum|date=6 January 2012|publisher=BBC|access-date=6 January 2012}} In early April 2012, the General Prosecutor's Office began examining the possible involvement of Tymoshenko and former prime minister Pavlo Lazarenko in the murder of Donetsk businessman Olexandr Momot in 1996.{{in lang|uk}} [https://www.bbc.com/ukrainian/politics/2012/04/120407_tymoshenko_momot_ko.shtml Генпрокуратура перевіряє Тимошенко на причетність до ще одного вбивства], [[BBC Ukrainian]] (7 April 2012) A trial concerning alleged misappropriating public funds of United Energy Systems of Ukraine started on 19 April 2012 in Kharkiv.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-17767451|title=Ukraine's Tymoshenko refuses to attend new tax trial|work=BBC News|date=19 April 2012|access-date=6 November 2015}} Tymoshenko refused to attend the trial, citing problems with her health. Tymoshenko was then moved against her will from [[Kachanivska prison]] to a hospital where she began a [[hunger strike]] on 20 April to protest – according to her lawyer [[Serhiy Vlasenko]] – "what is happening in the country and what is happening to her in prison."[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-17824709 Ukraine's jailed ex-PM Tymoshenko 'on hunger strik], [[BBC News]] (24 April 2012) She ended the hunger strike on 9 May 2012.[https://www.theglobeandmail.com/news/world/former-ukraine-pm-tymoshenko-moved-from-prison-to-hospital/article2427284/ Ukraine's Tymoshenko moved from prison to hospital; ends hunger strike], ''[[The Globe and Mail]]'' (9 May 2012) {{dead link|date=June 2016|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} Beginning on 9 May 2012, she received treatment at the hospital after being diagnosed with a [[spinal disc herniation]]. The Supreme Court of Ukraine upheld the verdict against Tymoshenko on 29 August 2012.{{cite news | url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB10000872396390443864204577619120814530212 | title=Ukraine's Highest Court Upholds Tymoshenko Verdict | work=[[The Wall Street Journal]] | agency=Associated Press | date=29 August 2012 | access-date=29 August 2012}}
[https://www.arkansasonline.com/news/2012/aug/29/ukraines-highest-court-upholds-tymoshenko-verdict/ Ukraine's highest court upholds Tymoshenko verdict], [[Arkansas Online]] (29 August 2012 ) From 29 October to 16 November 2012, Tymoshenko was again on a hunger strike to protest [[vote rigging]] in the October [[2012 Ukrainian parliamentary election]].{{cite news |url= https://www.kyivpost.com/content/ukraine/ukraines-jailed-tymoshenko-calls-off-hunger-strike-316252.html |title=Ukraine's jailed Tymoshenko calls off hunger strike |publisher=[[Kyiv Post]] |agency=Reuters |date=16 November 2012 |access-date=6 November 2015}}
{{cite news |url= https://www.kyivpost.com/content/ukraine/chief-doctor-tymoshenko-to-have-recovered-from-hunger-strike-by-end-of-this-week-316859.html |title=Chief doctor:Tymoshenko to have recovered from hunger strike by end of this week |publisher=[[Kyiv Post]] |agency=Interfax-Ukraine |date=28 November 2012 |access-date=6 November 2015}}[https://www.kyivpost.com/content/ukraine/tymoshenko-to-decide-whether-to-continue-hunger-strike-316055.html Tymoshenko to decide whether to continue hunger strike], ''[[Kyiv Post]]'' (13 November 2012) [[Fatherland United Opposition]] nominated Tymoshenko as its candidate for the [[2015 Ukrainian presidential election|Ukrainian presidential elections of 2015]] on 7 December 2012.[https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/united-opposition-nominates-tymoshenko-as-single-presidential-candidate-317296.html United Opposition nominates Tymoshenko as single presidential candidate], [[Kyiv Post]] (7 December 2012)
[https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/yatseniuk-tymoshenko-will-be-able-to-run-for-presidency-in-2015-317297.html Yatseniuk:Tymoshenko will be able to run for presidency in 2015], [[Kyiv Post]] (7 December 2012) On 14 June 2013, the congress of her party approved the decision to nominate her as its candidate in the 2015 Ukrainian presidential election.{{#tag:ref|In December 2010 Tymoshenko had stated she might [[2015 Ukrainian presidential election|run for President in 2015]], but that this also depended on her family.[https://web.archive.org/web/20110115005433/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/92568 Tymoshenko still ready to run for president], ''Kyiv Post'' (10 December 2010)|group=nb}} On 18 January 2013, Tymoshenko was notified that she was a suspect in the murder of businessman and lawmaker [[Yevhen Shcherban]], his wife and two other people in 1996.{{cite news |title= UKRAINIAN EX-PM SUSPECTED IN MURDER CASE |url= https://www.thejakartapost.com/news/2013/01/19/ukrainian-ex-pm-suspected-murder-case.html |agency= Associated Press |access-date= 19 January 2013 |url-status= dead |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20130121183748/https://www.thejakartapost.com/news/2013/01/19/ukrainian-ex-pm-suspected-murder-case.html |archive-date= 21 January 2013 |df= dmy-all}}
[https://www.euronews.com/2013/01/19/tymoshenko-accused-of-murder Tymoshenko accused of murder] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200918105535/https://www.euronews.com/2013/01/19/tymoshenko-accused-of-murder |date=18 September 2020}}, [[Euronews]] (19 January 2013)
[https://web.archive.org/web/20130518191834/https://www.montrealgazette.com/news/Jailed+Ukraine+exPM+Tymoshenko+suspected+longdormant+murder/7844225/story.html Jailed Ukraine ex-PM Tymoshenko suspected in long-dormant murder case], [[Montreal Gazette]] (19 January 2013)
[https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2013-01-18/ukraine-prosecutors-say-tymoshenko-may-face-murder-charge Ukraine Prosecutors Say Tymoshenko May Face Murder Charge], [[Bloomberg L.P.]] (18 January 2013)
[https://www.reuters.com/article/us-ukraine-tymoshenko-idUSBRE90H0W820130118 Ukraine prosecutor accuses Tymoshenko of murder], [[Reuters]] (18 January 2013) In May 2013, the Shcherban murder case was suspended.{{cite web|url= https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_12_07_2013_02|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20131004213404/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_12_07_2013_02|archive-date= 4 October 2013 |title= Defender: Shcherban murder case suspended. No evidence against Yulia Tymoshenko (video) |publisher= Tymoshenko.ua |access-date= 28 December 2013}} From 25 November to 6 December 2013 (during the [[Euromaidan]] protests), Tymoshenko was again on a hunger strike in protest of "President Yanukovych's reluctance to sign the [[Ukraine–European Union relations#Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (DCFTA)|DCFTA]]" on 6 December.[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/179852.html Tymoshenko stops hunger strike], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (6 December 2013)[https://www.euronews.com/2013/11/25/jailed-tymoshenko-begins-hunger-strike-over-ukraines-eu-u-turn/ Jailed Tymoshenko on hunger strike over EU U-turn by Ukraine] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200923005206/https://www.euronews.com/2013/11/25/jailed-tymoshenko-begins-hunger-strike-over-ukraines-eu-u-turn |date=23 September 2020}}, [[Euronews]] (25 November 2013)[https://www.euronews.com/2013/12/06/ukraine-opposition-leader-tymoshenko-ends-prison-hunger-strike/ Ukraine opposition leader Tymoshenko ends prison hunger strike], [[Euronews]] (6 December 2013)[https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-25267130 Russia and Ukraine leaders seek partnership treaty], [[BBC News]] (6 December 2013) ====Appeal==== [[File:Судять.jpg|thumb|Yulia Tymoshenko in 2011]] On 24 October 2011, Tymoshenko filed an appeal to the decision of Pechersk district court of Kyiv regarding the "gas case".{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/ukrainian/politics/2011/10/111024_tymoshenko_appeal_ak.shtml|title=Tymoshenko filed the appeal on a sentence on "gas business"|publisher= Bbc.com|date=24 October 2011|access-date=24 October 2011}} On 1 December, the Kyiv Court of Appeal started hearing the case. Tymoshenko herself was not present in the courtroom because of her health condition.{{cite news|url=https://www.radiosvoboda.mobi/a/24408285.html|title=The court considered possible a preliminary review of the complaint Tymoshenko without her|newspaper=Радіо Свобода |publisher= Radio Svoboda|date=1 December 2011|access-date=1 December 2011 }} After the hearing, the judge, Olena Sitaylo, had to call an ambulance and was hospitalized. On 13 December 2011, the Kyiv Court of Appeal resumed the hearing. All subsequent court sessions took place without Tymoshenko's presence. Immediately prior to the hearing of the appeal, the board of judges was altered: Sitaylo, the chief justice, was appointed the day before the first hearing; other justices were appointed several days prior to the court session.{{cite web|url=https://novaukraina.org/news/urn:news:AA7F7D|title=The trial of Tymoshenko will continue "in any weather"|publisher=Novaukraina.org|date=20 December 2011|access-date=20 December 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120105224441/https://novaukraina.org/news/urn:news:AA7F7D|archive-date=5 January 2012|url-status=dead}} Thus, the judges did not have time to study the 84-page case log. The manner of the process proved that the decision to alter the board of judges was made beforehand. At the very end, Tymoshenko's defense boycotted the court session.{{cite web|url=https://fakty.ictv.ua/ua/index/read-news/id/1437911|title=Protection of Tymoshenko will not go to court|publisher=Fakty.ictv.ua|date=22 December 2011|access-date=22 December 2011|archive-date=4 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112424/http://fakty.ictv.ua/ua/index/read-news/id/1437911|url-status=dead}} On 23 December 2011, the Kyiv Court of Appeal issued a ruling which fully supported the verdict of the Pechersk court. The judges didn't find any violations during the pre-trial investigation or trial on the "gas case", overruling the claims of Tymoshenko's defense.{{cite web|url=https://en.for-ua.com/news/2011/12/14/125211.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120709040426/https://en.for-ua.com/news/2011/12/14/125211.html|archive-date=9 July 2012 |title=Court of Appeals leaves Yulia Tymoshenko in custody |publisher=En.for-ua.com |date=14 December 2011 |access-date=28 December 2013}} ====Cassation==== On 26 January 2012, Tymoshenko's defense submitted a [[Court of cassation|cassation appeal]] to the High Specialized Court for Civil and Criminal Cases regarding the "gas case" verdict.{{cite web|url=https://en.for-ua.com/news/2012/01/26/144035.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120709112624/https://en.for-ua.com/news/2012/01/26/144035.html|archive-date=9 July 2012 |title=Tymoshenko's defense submitted cassation appeal against "gas sentence" |publisher=En.for-ua.com |date=26 January 2012 |access-date=28 December 2013}} On 16 August 2012, after a 7-month delay that impeded filing the case to the European Court of Human Rights, the panel of judges of the aforementioned court began hearing the case. The panel finished hearing the case on 21 August and went to the jury room to make decision. The ruling of the Court, issued on 29 August 2012, stated that the appeal of former prime minister Yulia Tymoshenko's defense on the "gas case" should not be satisfied.{{cite web|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/court-reads-out-cassation-complaints-against-tymoshenkos-gas-case-verdict-311681.html |title=Tymoshenko's cassation in gas case read out in court |publisher=Kyivpost.com |date=17 August 2012 |access-date=28 December 2013}} Media, diplomats, members of parliament and members of an EU special monitoring mission, Pat Cox and Aleksander Kwaśniewski, attended the court sessions. The ruling was announced on the day following public hearing of "Tymoshenko vs Ukraine" (regarding unlawful arrest of ex-prime minister and holding her in custody) case at the European Court of Human Rights.{{cite web|url=https://www.rbc.ua/ukr/analytics/espch-nachal-publichnye-slushaniya-po-delu-timoshenko-protiv-28082012143100|title=The European Court of human rights has completed its public hearings in the case "Tymoshenko against Ukraine"|publisher= Rbc.ua|date=28 August 2012|access-date=28 August 2012}} The European Union, PACE, and governments of the United States, Great Britain and Canada expressed frustration with the cassation ruling.{{cite web|url=https://www.day.kyiv.ua/en/article/day-after-day/staunch-court |title=A staunch court |publisher=Day.kyiv.ua |date=4 September 2012 |access-date=28 December 2013}} "We are deeply disappointed with the consequences of the current situation, when two important opposition leaders cannot stand in the upcoming parliamentary elections, [and] when the court disrespects international standards for fair and transparent processes", a representative of the European Commission, Michael Mann, said in Brussels on 29 August 2012.{{cite web|url=https://old.nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/509412/|title=EU deeply disappointed by court ruling on Tymoshenko's appeal against gas case verdict|publisher=Old.nrcu.gov.ua|date=29 August 2012|access-date=29 August 2012|archive-date=20 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151120031811/http://old.nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/509412/|url-status=dead}} ====International reactions==== The "gas case" trial was viewed by many European and American organizations as a politically charged persecution that violates the law.{{cite web|url= https://helsinki.org.ua/files/docs/1321265218.pdf |title=Legal Monitoring in Ukraine III: Preliminary Report on the investigations against Yulia Tymoshenko in November 2011 |work=The Danish Helsinki Committee for Human Rights|date=8 November 2011 |access-date=7 November 2015}}{{cite web |first=Pieter |last=Omtzigt |url=https://www.assembly.coe.int/Communication/ajdoc15_2013.pdf |title=Committee on Legal Affairs and Human Rights: Keeping political and criminal responsibility separate (draft resolution) |publisher=Council of Europe Parliamentary Assembly |date=23 April 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20131104025241/https://www.assembly.coe.int/Communication/ajdoc15_2013.pdf |archive-date=4 November 2013}}{{cite web |url= https://www.echr.coe.int/sites/search_eng/pages/search.aspx#{%22fulltext%22:[%22Tymoshenko%22]} |title=ECHR |publisher=Echr.coe.int |access-date=26 July 2014}}{{cite web|last=Spillius |first=Alex |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/10324152/EU-to-Kiev-free-Yulia-Tymoshenko-or-no-pact.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220112/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/10324152/EU-to-Kiev-free-Yulia-Tymoshenko-or-no-pact.html |archive-date=12 January 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |title=EU to Kiev: free Yulia Tymoshenko, or no pact |date=20 September 2013 |newspaper=Telegraph |access-date=28 December 2013}}{{cbignore}}{{cite news |url= https://news.liga.net/news/politics/676066-zakharov_zayavil_chto_timoshenko_bezuslovno_politzaklyuchennaya.htm |script-title=ru:Захаров заявил, что Тимошенко – безусловно политзаключенная |trans-title=Zakharov declared that Tymoshenko – is undoubtedly a political prisoner |language=ru|work=LIGA.net|date=7 June 2012 |access-date=7 November 2015}}{{cite web|url=https://khpg.org/index.php?id=1313446474 |title=Danish Helsinki Committee: Legal Monitoring in Ukraine II, Full Text |publisher=Khpg.org |access-date=28 December 2013}} The EU and multiple international organizations see the conviction as "justice being applied selectively under political motivation."{{cite news |url=https://www.euronews.com/2011/10/11/eu-feels-let-down-by-ukraine-over-tymoshenko/ |title=EU feels let down by Ukraine over Tymoshenko |work=[[Euronews]] |date=11 October 2011 |access-date=6 November 2015 |archive-date=24 September 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924003713/http://www.euronews.com/2011/10/11/eu-feels-let-down-by-ukraine-over-tymoshenko/ |url-status=dead}} In June 2012, the European Parliament established a special monitoring mission to Ukraine, conducted by former European Parliament President Pat Cox and former Polish President Aleksander Kwaśniewski. Both politicians observed trials, repeatedly visited Tymoshenko in custody and conducted meetings with Ukraine's authorities regarding her release.{{cite web|url=https://ipress.ua/news/koks_i_kvasnievskyy_pryihaly_do_tymoshenko_v_likarnyu_21755.html|title=The mission of the European Parliament under the slogan "Julia will" have arrived to Timoshenko|publisher= Ipress.ua|date=15 June 2013|access-date=15 June 2013}} The European Union shelved the [[EU-Ukraine relations#Ukraine–European Union Association Agreement|European Union Association Agreement]] and [[EU-Ukraine relations#Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (DCFTA)|Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Agreement]] with the Ukrainian government over the issue.{{cite web|author=Yulia Tymoshenko sentenced |url=https://www.osw.waw.pl/en/publikacje/analyses/2011-10-12/yulia-tymoshenko-sentenced |title=Yulia Tymoshenko sentenced |publisher=OSW |date=12 October 2011 |access-date=28 December 2013}}{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/eu-leaders-ratification-of-association-agreement-a-310272.html|title=EU leaders:Ratification of Association Agreement and DCFTA depends on settlement of Tymoshenko-Lutsenko issue|publisher=Kyiv Post|agency=Interfax-Ukraine|date=20 July 2012}} On 30 April 2013, the European Court of Human Rights issued a judgment{{cite web|url=https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/sites/fra-press/pages/search.aspx?i=003-4343134-5208270 |title=HUDOC Search Page |publisher=Hudoc.echr.coe.int |access-date=28 December 2013}} asserting that "Ms. Tymoshenko's pre-trial detention had been arbitrary; that the lawfulness of her detention had not been properly reviewed; and, that she had no possibility to seek compensation for her unlawful deprivation of liberty." [[Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe]] (PACE) has adopting a resolution on "Keeping political and criminal responsibility separate" in which former prime minister of Ukraine Yulia Tymoshenko is recognized as a political prisoner.{{cite web|url=https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_02_07_2013_01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130709095208/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_02_07_2013_01|archive-date=9 July 2013 |title=PACE: Yulia Tymoshenko is a political prisoner|publisher=tymoshenko.ua |access-date=2 July 2013}} The United States Senate passed two resolutions calling for the release from prison of former prime minister Tymoshenko. The most recent, presented in the Senate in June 2013, called for Tymoshenko's release in light of the recent European Court of Human Rights ruling, and was adopted on 18 November 2013.{{cite web|url=https://www.congress.gov/bill/113th-congress/senate-resolution/165/text |title=United States Senate Resolution on Ukraine. Full Text |date=18 November 2013 |publisher=Beta.congress.gov |access-date=28 December 2013}}{{cite web|url=https://un.ua/eng/article/477245.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131119102528/https://un.ua/eng/article/477245.html|archive-date=19 November 2013 |title=U.S. Senate Adopts Resolution Demanding Ukraine Release Tymoshenko Under European Court of Human Rights' Ruling on Her Illegal Detention And Arrest |publisher=Un.ua |date=20 November 2013 |access-date=28 December 2013}}{{cite web|author=Committee on Foreign Relations |url=https://www.govtrack.us/congress/bills/113/sres165/text |title=US Congress Resolution 165 |publisher=Govtrack.us |access-date=28 December 2013}} An earlier resolution, passed in 2012, condemned the politically motivated prosecution and imprisonment of former prime minister of Ukraine Yulia Tymoshenko.{{cite web |url=https://www.opencongress.org/bill/sres466-112/show |title=US Congress Resolution 466 |publisher=Opencongress.org |access-date=28 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131031020123/https://www.opencongress.org/bill/sres466-112/show |archive-date=31 October 2013}} On 2 October 2013, the PACE adopted a resolution calling for the immediate release of Tymoshenko and, two days later, [[Pat Cox]] and [[Aleksander Kwaśniewski]], representatives of the [[European Parliament]] mission, handed president Yanukovych a petition to pardon Tymoshenko.{{cite web|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/pace-calls-for-tymoshenko-release-330035.html |title=PACE calls for Tymoshenko release |publisher=Kyivpost.com |date=2 October 2013 |access-date=28 December 2013}} {{cite web |url= https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-politics/1556262-cox_kwasniewski_asking_yanukovych_to_pardon_tymoshenko_310607.html |title=Cox, Kwasniewski asking Yanukovych to pardon Tymoshenko |publisher=Ukrinform.ua |date=4 October 2013 |access-date=28 December 2013 }} On 21 February 2014, Parliament voted to allow the release of former Prime Minister Yulia Tymoshenko, who has been imprisoned for more than two years. In December 2012, the united opposition nominated her and later in June 2013 confirmed her as its candidate in the 2015 Ukrainian presidential election.[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/157239.html Batkivschyna to nominate Tymoshenko for presidency, Yatseniuk heads party's political council], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (14 June 2013) ==== Aftermath — Manafort case ==== [[File:Meeting Clinton.jpg|thumb|Meeting between Tymoshenko and [[United States Secretary of State|US Secretary of State]] [[Hillary Clinton]] (Kyiv, June 2, 2010)]] According to the September 2018 indictment in which [[Paul Manafort]] confessed as part of a plea bargain with [[U.S.]] [[special prosecutor]] [[Robert Mueller]], Manafort and his partner [[Tony Podesta]], brother of [[Hillary Clinton]]'s campaign manager [[John Podesta]], helped the former Ukrainian President to conduct a media campaign in the West directed against Tymoshenko in order to undermine the support for her by [[Presidency of Barack Obama|the administration]] of then U.S. President [[Barack Obama]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2018/apr/05/ex-trump-aide-paul-manafort-approved-black-ops-to-help-ukraine-president|title=Ex-Trump aide Paul Manafort approved 'black ops' to help Ukraine president|last=Harding|first=Luke|date=5 April 2018|work=The Guardian|access-date=6 April 2018|language=en-GB|issn=0261-3077}} In exchange for his testimony against Manafort, Mueller gave Tony Podesta and The Podesta Group complete amnesty, including not prosecuting them for being unregistered agents of a foreign government.{{Cite news|url=https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/news/robert-mueller-offers-tony-podesta-immunity-to-testify-against-paul-manafort-report|title=Robert Mueller offers Tony Podesta immunity to testify against Paul Manafort: Report|last=Chaiten|first=Daniel|date=30 July 2018|work=The Washington Examiner|access-date=30 July 2018|language=en-GB}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.politico.com/story/2017/04/paul-manafort-lobbying-ukraine-podesta-group-237163|title=Podesta Group Files Disclosures After Being Offered Immunity In Exchange For Testifying Against Manafort|last=Arnsdorf|first=Isaac|date=12 April 2017|work=Politico|access-date=12 April 2017|language=en-GB}} The campaign was designed to make Tymoshenko look like a supporter of [[anti-Semitism]].{{cite news |title=Manafort's Mysterious Israeli Connection: Everything We Know So Far |date=2018-09-18 |work=Haaretz |url=https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/2018-09-18/ty-article/manaforts-mysterious-israeli-connection-everything-we-know-so-far/0000017f-f333-dc28-a17f-ff372cac0000 |access-date=2018-10-07}} The indictment also states that in July 2011, former U.S. journalist [[Alan Friedman]] sent Manafort a confidential six-page document entitled "Ukraine – the digital road map", which contained a plan for "destruction" of Tymoshenko using video, articles and social networks.[https://m.huffpost.com/us/entry/us_5b9c2e61e4b046313fbb2867 Paul Manafort's Plea Deal Could Be Bad News For A Bunch Of Washington Operatives], [[HuffPost]] (14 September 2018) The plan included creating a website, posting on the Internet, and sending out e-mails to "the target audience in Europe and the U.S." It was also proposed to edit the page of Yulia Tymoshenko in Wikipedia in order to emphasize the "corruption and legal proceedings" related to her. ===2014 release from prison=== [[File:Yulia Tymoshenko addressing Euromaidan with a speech. Kyiv, Ukraine. Events of February 22, 2014..jpg|thumb|Tymoshenko addressing [[Euromaidan]] with a speech, Kyiv, 22 February 2014]] Following the [[Revolution of Dignity]], on 21 February 2014, Parliament voted for her release in a 310–54 veto-proof vote. To do so, the members of parliament decriminalized the Article on which Tymoshenko was charged and brought it into compliance with Article 19 of the UN Convention against corruption. That could enable immediate release of Tymoshenko through the corresponding court ruling. However, Viktor Yanukovych fled the country after massive violent clashes in Kyiv that killed more than 80 people{{cite news|first1=Maria|last1=Danilova|first2=Yuras|last2=Karmanau|url=https://www.ctvnews.ca/world/article/tensions-high-at-kyiv-protest-camp-despite-ground-breaking-deal/|title=Tensions high at Kyiv protest camp despite ground-breaking deal|agency=[[CTV News]]|date=21 February 2014|access-date=6 November 2015}} without signing the bill into law. On 22 February 2014, the Verkhovna Rada with 322 votes adopted a decree based on the decision of the European Court of Human Rights and corresponding decision of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe.{{cite news |url= https://www.voanews.com/a/ukraines-tymoshenko-declares-presidential-candidacy/1880823.html |title=Ukraine's Tymoshenko Declares Presidential Candidacy |publisher=[[Voice of America]] |date=27 March 2014 |access-date=6 November 2015}}{{cite news |url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-26312008 |title=Ukraine: Speaker Oleksandr Turchynov named interim president |agency= [[BBC News]] |date=23 February 2014 |access-date=6 November 2015}}{{cite news |url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-26248275 |title=Ukraine protests timeline |agency=[[BBC News]] |date=23 February 2014 |access-date=6 November 2015}}{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/ukraine/coe-committee-of-ministers-urges-ukrainian-government-to-compensate-tymoshenko-for-violated-rights-333190.html|title=Council of Europe Committee of Ministers urges Ukrainian government to compensate Tymoshenko for violated rights|publisher=Kyivpost.com|agency=Interfax-Ukraine|date=6 December 2013|access-date=6 November 2015}} On the same day, Tymoshenko was released from Central Clinical Hospital No. 5 in [[Kharkiv]], where she had been receiving treatment under police guard since May 2012, after being diagnosed with a spinal disc herniation.{{cite news|first=Shaun|last=Walker|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2014/feb/23/yulia-tymoshenko-returns-ukraine-politics|title=Yulia Tymoshenko is back centre stage in Ukraine – but not all want her there|work=The Guardian|date=24 February 2014|location=Kyiv|access-date=6 November 2015}}{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/opposition-leaders-visit-tymoshenkos-hospital-316059.html|title=Opposition leaders visit Tymoshenko's hospital|publisher=[[Kyiv Post]]|date=13 November 2012|location=Kharkiv|access-date=24 February 2014}}
{{Citation |url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/german-doctors-arrive-at-kharkiv-based-clinic-to-see-tymoshenko-316141.html |title=German doctors arrive at Kharkiv-based clinic to see Tymoshenko |journal=[[Kyiv Post]] |location=Kharkiv |date=15 November 2012}}
{{Citation |url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/world/rome-mayors-office-puts-up-tymoshenko-portrait-on-facade-316747.html |title=Rome mayor's office puts up Tymoshenko portrait on facade |newspaper=[[Kyiv Post]] |date=26 November 2012}}
{{Citation |url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/191607.html |title=Ukraine opposition leader demands closure of protesters' cases, Tymoshenko release |newspaper=[[Interfax-Ukraine]] |date=21 February 2014 |access-date=24 February 2014}}{{Citation |url=https://rapsinews.com/judicial_news/20120831/264475915.html |title=France disappointed by Tymoshenko appeal rejection |newspaper=rapsinews.com |date=31 August 2012 |location=Paris}} Her release was praised by western leaders.{{cite web|url=https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_23_02_2014_06|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140307071749/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_23_02_2014_06|archive-date=7 March 2014 |title=Yulia Tymoshenko meets with EU and U.S. diplomats (photos) – Yulia Tymoshenko. Official Website |publisher=Tymoshenko.ua |access-date=26 July 2014}} On 28 February 2014, the parliament rehabilitated Yulia Tymoshenko and restored her rights.{{cite news |title=Embattled Ukraine President Signs Compromise Deal as Parliament Votes to Free His Imprisoned Rival |url= https://www.nytimes.com/2014/02/22/world/europe/ukraine.html?emc=edit_na_20140221&_r=0 |access-date=21 February 2014 |newspaper=New York Times |date=21 February 2014 |first1=Andrew |last1=Higgins |first2=Andrew |last2=Cramer |url-access=registration}}{{cite web |url= https://www.nbcnews.com/storyline/ukraine-crisis/ally-opposition-icon-tymoshenko-voted-acting-ukraine-president-n36531 |title=Ally of Opposition Icon Tymoshenko Voted Acting Ukraine President |work=NBC News |date=23 February 2014 |access-date=25 February 2014}} That enabled her to run for office; however, she has ruled out becoming prime minister again.{{cite news|url=https://www.malaysiasun.com/index.php/sid/220190358|title=President Yanukovych and Ukraine opposition sign early poll deal|publisher=[[The Sun (Malaysia)]]|date=21 February 2014|location=Kyiv|access-date=24 February 2014|archive-date=10 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141110140143/http://www.malaysiasun.com/index.php/sid/220190358|url-status=dead}} Kyivsky District Court of Kharkiv closed the criminal case on financial abuse of the United Energy Systems of Ukraine on 28 February 2014.[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/193370.html Kharkiv court drops charges against Tymoshenko in UESU case], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (28 February 2014) And, on 14 April, the Supreme Court of Ukraine closed the "gas case" against Tymoshenko for "absence of a criminal act".{{cite news|url=https://tass.ru/en/world/729631|title=Ukrainian Prosecutor General's Office opens case vs. judges who sentenced Tymoshenko|publisher=[[ITAR-TASS]]|date=25 April 2014|access-date=6 November 2015}}{{cite news|url=https://tass.ru/en/world/727757|title=Ukrainian Supreme Court closes Tymoshenko's 'gas case'|publisher=[[ITAR-TASS]]|date=14 April 2014|access-date=6 November 2015}} On 25 April 2014, the General Prosecutor of Ukraine launched a pre-trial investigation against a number of officials from its own office and the Pechersky district court and Kyiv's court of appeals (the judges who had sentenced Tymoshenko) because of allegedly "deliberate, systematic and flagrant violation of accused Yulia Tymoshenko's rights to defense, which are granted by Ukraine's current laws". On 24 June 2014, the Supreme Court of Ukraine rehabilitated Tymoshenko.{{cite news|url=https://vestnikkavkaza.net/news/politics/56829.html|title=Ukrainian Supreme Court rehabilitates Julia Tymoshenko|work=Vestnikkavkaza|date=24 June 2014|access-date=1 March 2022|archive-date=8 March 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210308062403/https://vestnikkavkaza.net/news/politics/56829.html|url-status=dead}}{{cite news |url= https://tass.ru/en/world/727757 |title=Ukrainian Supreme Court closes Tymoshenko's 'gas case' |work=ITAR-TASS |date=14 April 2014 |access-date=26 July 2014}}{{cite web|url=https://www.humanrightseurope.org/2015/01/ukraine-court-discontinues-examination-of-tymoshenko-human-rights-complaint/|title=Ukraine: Court "discontinues" examination of Tymoshenko human rights complaint|publisher=humanrightseurope.org|access-date=22 January 2015|archive-date=28 June 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150628064756/http://www.humanrightseurope.org/2015/01/ukraine-court-discontinues-examination-of-tymoshenko-human-rights-complaint/|url-status=dead}}{{cite web |url= https://www.dw.com/en/echr-tymoshenko-suit-over-ukraine-treatment-settled/a-18208499 |title=ECHR:Tymoshenko suit over Ukraine treatment settled |publisher=dw.com|date=22 January 2015 |access-date=6 November 2015}} On 22 January 2015, the European Court of Human Rights announced the termination of consideration of the case of ''Yulia Tymoshenko v. Ukraine'' in connection to an implied [[friendly settlement]] between the parties, after Tymoshenko's agreement with the government's declaration admitting that the criminal prosecution against her had been politically motivated. The Ukrainian government, in its exchange with the Court, had acknowledged a violation of Tymoshenko's rights guaranteed by the European Convention on Human Rights under articles 3 (prohibition of torture), 6 ([[right to a fair trial]]), 7 (no punishment without law), 8 (right to respect for private and family life), partly in conjunction with article 13 ([[right to an effective remedy]]), article 18 (political motivation), article 10 (freedom of expression) and article 4 of Protocol No. 7 to the convention (right not to be tried or punished twice). Taking note of the Ukrainian government's declaration and following measures, as well as of Tymoshenko's agreement with it, the Court struck out the application from its list of cases, as asked by the government, following article 39 of the convention (friendly settlements).{{cite web|url=https://hudoc.echr.coe.int/app/conversion/pdf/?library=ECHR&id=003-4988882-6120225&filename=Decision%20Tymoshenko%20v.%20Ukraine%20%28no.%202%29%20-%20examination%20discontinued.pdf|title=European Court discontinues examination of second case brought by former Ukrainian Prime Minister Tymoshenko|publisher=ECHR Press Release|date=22 January 2015}} On 19 October 2015, the Kominternivskyi district court of Kharkiv has sentenced two former employees of the Kachanivska penal colony No. 54 to three years in prison for inflicting injuries on Tymoshenko.{{cite news|url=https://censor.net.ua/en/news/356901/tymoshenkos_jailers_sentenced_to_three_years_in_prison|title=Tymoshenko's jailers sentenced to three years in prison|newspaper=Censor.NET|language=en|date=20 October 2015|access-date=31 March 2019}} ===Political activities after release=== Immediately after her release from prison on 22 February 2014, Yulia Tymoshenko travelled to Kyiv, where she attended a makeshift memorial to the first slain protesters on Hrushevskogo Street and gave a speech on Maidan stage.{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/10656391/Freed-Yulia-Tymoshenko-praises-heroes-and-liberators.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140223230515/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/ukraine/10656391/Freed-Yulia-Tymoshenko-praises-heroes-and-liberators.html |url-status=dead |archive-date=23 February 2014 |title=Video: Freed Yulia Tymoshenko praises 'heroes and liberators' |newspaper=Telegraph |date=23 February 2014 |access-date=26 July 2014}} In the following days, she had a number of meetings and phone conversations with USA, EU, and OSCE officials. Tymoshenko addressed the European Union, leaders of western democracies and of countries which guaranteed Ukraine's territorial unity according to the Budapest Memorandum; she called for action to stop what she called the "Russian aggression".{{cite web |first=Maria |last=Danilova |url=https://huffingtonpost.com/2014/03/05/tymoshenko-west-must-stop-russia_n_4906708.html |title=Tymoshenko: West Must Stop Russia's Aggression |publisher=Huffingtonpost.com |access-date=26 July 2014 |archive-date=8 August 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140808075828/http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2014/03/05/tymoshenko-west-must-stop-russia_n_4906708.html |url-status=dead}} From 6 to 7 March, Tymoshenko attended a political conference of the European People's Party in Dublin, where she openly discussed events with [[Angela Merkel]], [[Jose Manuel Barroso]], [[Viviane Reding]], [[Michel Barnier]], [[Mariano Rajoy]] and [[Donald Tusk]], amongst other notable figures.{{cite news|first=Suzanne|last=Lynch|url=https://www.irishtimes.com/news/politics/tymoshenko-among-kiev-uprising-figures-to-visit-dublin-1.1712132|title=Tymoshenko among Kiev uprising figures to visit Dublin|newspaper=The Irish Times|date=4 March 2014|access-date=7 November 2015}} On 7 March 2014, she was admitted to the Charité hospital in Berlin, Germany, for treatment of her severe back problems.{{cite news|url=https://www.morgenpost.de/berlin-aktuell/article125568742/Timoschenko-in-Berlin-Charite-Aerzte-bereiten-Behandlung-vor.html|title=Timoschenko in Berlin – Charité-Ärzte bereiten Behandlung vor|trans-title=Tymoshenko in Berlin – Charité-Ärzte doctors prepare for treatment|language=de|work=Berliner Morgenpost|date=7 March 2014|access-date=7 November 2015}} Upon her return to Kyiv, Tymoshenko gathered military and defense experts and suggested launching a special headquarters that would elaborate responses to threats coming from Russia. [[File:Tymoshenko 2014(PR).png|thumb|2014 presidential election percentage of vote for Tymoshenko]] On 27 March 2014, at a press conference in Kyiv, Tymoshenko stated that she would run in the [[2014 Ukrainian presidential election|2014 presidential election]].{{cite news|first1=Patrick|last1=Reevell|first2=David M.|last2=Herszenhorn|first3=Steven|last3=Erlanger|author-link3=Steven Erlanger|first4=Rick|last4=Gladstone|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2014/03/28/world/europe/ukraine.html?_r=1|title=Former Prime Minister Announces Candidacy for President of Ukraine|work=The New York Times|date=27 March 2014|access-date=7 November 2015}} Two days later, the congress of Batkivshchyna party officially nominated her and on 31 March the Central Election Commission officially registered her as a candidate. The key theses of Tymoshenko's election campaign were the eradication of corruption, the fight against oligarchs, the European path of Ukraine's development (in particular, the signing of the Association Agreement with the EU), countering Russian aggression and restoring the territorial integrity of Ukraine.{{cite web|url=https://www.cvk.gov.ua/pls/vp2014/wp001|title=Election of the President of Ukraine 2014 Yulia Tymoshenko. Pre-election program|publisher=CVK|date=31 March 2014}} The election took place on 25 May. Tymoshenko came a distant second behind Petro Poroshenko. She received 12.39% of the vote.{{cite web|url= https://www.nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/566632/ |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20140529212731/https://nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/566632/ |archive-date=29 May 2014 |title=Poroshenko wins presidential election with 54.7% of vote – CEC |publisher=[[Radio Ukraine International]] |date=29 May 2014}}{{cite news|first=David|last=Stern|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-27569057|title=Petro Poroshenko claims Ukraine presidency|publisher=BBC News Online|date=25 May 2014|access-date=26 May 2014}}{{cite news|url=https://www.pravdareport.com/world/ussr/26-05-2014/127649-chocolate_king_poroshenko-0/|title=Chocolate King Poroshenko beats Orange Princess Tymoshenko|publisher=[[Pravda.ru]]|date=26 May 2014|access-date=6 November 2015|archive-date=9 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180126/http://www.pravdareport.com/world/ussr/26-05-2014/127649-chocolate_king_poroshenko-0/|url-status=dead}} On 30 August 2014, Tymoshenko announced her Batkivshchina party would start gathering signatures to trigger a referendum on NATO accession.{{cite web|url=https://tass.ru/en/world/747378|title=Tymoshenko announces start of preparations for NATO accession referendum|publisher=ITAR-TASS|date=30 August 2014|access-date=30 August 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220301144457/https://tass.com/world/747378 |archive-date=1 March 2022}} In the [[2014 Ukrainian parliamentary election]], "Fatherland" received 5.68% of the vote and 19 seats in parliament.{{cite news |url= https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-politics/1726373-poroshenko_bloc_to_have_greatest_number_of_seats_in_parliament_327072.html |title=Poroshenko Bloc to have greatest number of seats in parliament |publisher=[[Ukrinform]] |date=8 November 2014 |access-date=6 November 2015}} In the elections, Tymoshenko was placed second on the parties electoral list, after [[Nadiya Savchenko]].{{in lang|uk}} [https://web.archive.org/web/20140915212430/https://tvi.ua/new/2014/09/15/povnyy_vyborchyy_spysok_partiyi_batkivschyna Full electoral list of "Fatherland"], [[TVi (TV channel)|TVi]] (15 September 2014) After the election, Tymoshenko again became faction leader.[https://web.archive.org/web/20141128134758/https://nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/583715/ Six factions formed in parliament], [[National Radio Company of Ukraine]] (27 November 2014) She is a member of the [[Committee of the Verkhovna Rada on issues of European integration]] in the 8th convocation of parliament.{{cite web|title=Committee on issues of European integration|url=https://w1.c1.rada.gov.ua/pls/site2/p_komity_list?pidid=2626|website=Official portal|publisher=[[Verkhovna Rada|Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine]]|access-date=10 December
