2014|language=uk}} After the election, Tymoshenko began reforming the Batkivshchyna party.{{cite web|url=https://censor.net.ua/news/288154/timoshenko_anonsirovala_provedenie_reformy_v_batkivschine_rabotayu_nad_reformoyi_partii|title=Tymoshenko announced reform in "Fatherland": "I am working on the reform of the party"|work=Цензор.НЕТ |publisher=Сensor.net|date=4 February 2014 |last1=Нет |first1=Цензор }} === Elections of the President of Ukraine === On March 27, 2014, at a press conference in Kyiv, Yulia Tymoshenko announced her decision to run for president.{{Cite web |date=2014-03-28 |title=Юлія Тимошенко: я балотуватимуся на посаду президента на виборах 25 травня (фото, відео) - Юлія Тимошенко. Офіційний сайт. |url=http://www.tymoshenko.ua/uk/article/yulia_tymoshenko_27_03_2014_01 |access-date=2023-12-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140328204146/http://www.tymoshenko.ua/uk/article/yulia_tymoshenko_27_03_2014_01 |archive-date=28 March 2014 }} On March 29, the "Batkivshchyna" congress, which took place on Sofia Maidan, nominated her for the post of President of Ukraine.{{Cite web |date=2014-04-02 |title="Батьківщина" висунула Тимошенко в президенти - "Факти" |url=http://fakty.ictv.ua/ua/index/read-news/id/1509882 |access-date=2023-12-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140402223947/http://fakty.ictv.ua/ua/index/read-news/id/1509882 |archive-date=2 April 2014 }} The key theses of Tymoshenko's pre-election program were the eradication of corruption, the fight against oligarchs, the European path of Ukraine's development (in particular, the signing of the Association Agreement with the EU), countering Russian aggression, and restoring Ukraine's territorial integrity.{{Cite web |date=2018-02-27 |title= |url=http://www.cvk.gov.ua/pls/vp2014/wp001 |access-date=2023-12-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180227134347/http://www.cvk.gov.ua/pls/vp2014/wp001 |archive-date=27 February 2018 }} In order to win the presidential elections and the subsequent political struggle, Ukrainian presidential candidate Yulia Tymoshenko hired political technologist Tal Zilbershtein, who once advised ex-Prime Minister of Israel Ehud Olmert, to her team.{{Cite web |date=2014-05-11 |title=Війна штабів. Навіщо їй участь, а йому - перемога |url=http://news.liga.net/ua/articles/politics/1658512-v_yna_shtab_v_nav_shcho_y_uchast_a_yomu_peremoga.htm |access-date=2023-12-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140511070411/http://news.liga.net/ua/articles/politics/1658512-v_yna_shtab_v_nav_shcho_y_uchast_a_yomu_peremoga.htm |archive-date=11 May 2014 }} Yulia Tymoshenko took second place with a result of 12.81% in the extraordinary elections of the President of Ukraine,{{Cite web |last=FÄRM |first=Göran |date= |title=ELECTION OBSERVATION DELEGATION TO THE PRESIDENTIAL ELECTIONS IN UKRAINE |url=https://www.europarl.europa.eu/cmsdata/212623/Election_report_Ukraine_25_May_2014_.pdf}}{{cite web |title=Цен тральної в иборчо ї комі сі ї: Про результати виборів президента україни |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140625061215/http://www.cvk.gov.ua/info/protokol_cvk_25052014.pdf |archive-date=2014-06-25 |url-status=dead |url=http://www.cvk.gov.ua/info/protokol_cvk_25052014.pdf |date=2014-05-25 |trans-title=On the results of the presidential elections of Ukraine |language=uk |access-date=2023-12-29 |website=Central Election Commission of Ukraine}} which took place in one round on May 25, 2014. Petro Poroshenko won the election. After the elections, Tymoshenko began reforming the Batkivshchyna party.{{citation needed|date=January 2024}} ===Parliamentary activity, 2014–2018=== On 11 December 2014, the Rada supported Tymoshenko's initiative on freeing Nadiya Savchenko.{{cite web|url= https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/news-en/rada-supports-yulia-tymoshenko-s-initiative-on-freeing-nadiya-savchenko/|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20150721125452/https://www2.tymoshenko.ua/en/news-en/rada-supports-yulia-tymoshenko-s-initiative-on-freeing-nadiya-savchenko/|archive-date= 21 July 2015|title= Rada supports Yulia Tymoshenko's initiative on freeing Nadiya Savchenko|work= Yulia Tymoshenko's official site|date= 11 December 2014|access-date= 7 November 2015}} On 5 March 2015, Parliament supported a bill to support the volunteer movement in Ukraine.{{cite web|url= https://samoorg.com.ua/blog/2015/03/06/verhovna-rada-pidtrimala-dovgoochikuvani-zmini-do-zakonu-pro-volonterstvo/|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20150518115850/https://samoorg.com.ua/blog/2015/03/06/verhovna-rada-pidtrimala-dovgoochikuvani-zmini-do-zakonu-pro-volonterstvo/|archive-date= 18 May 2015|script-title= uk:Верховна Рада підтримала довгоочікувані зміни до закону про волонтерство|trans-title= Supreme Council supported the long-awaited changes to the law on volunteering|language=uk|work= samoorg.com.ua|date= 6 March 2015|access-date= 7 November 2015}} On 21 April 2015, Tymoshenko initiated a working group to check the validity of utility tariffs.{{cite news|first= Oleksandr|last= Yermochenko|url= https://www.unn.com.ua/uk/news/1458809-u-radi-stvorili-robochu-grupu-schodo-perevirki-obgruntovanosti-tarifiv-na-komunalni-poslugi|script-title= uk:У Раді створили робочу групу з перевірки обгрунтованості комунальних тарифів|trans-title= The Rada has established a working group to check the validity of utility tariffs|language=uk|publisher= UNN news|date= 21 April 2015|access-date= 6 November 2015}} On 6 April 2016, Tymoshenko thanked Angela Merkel for her help in establishing peace in eastern Ukraine.{{cite web | url= https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/news-en/we-are-grateful-to-angela-merkel-for-her-leadership-in-the-ukraine-peace-process/ | title= We are grateful to Angela Merkel | work= Yulia Tymoshenko's official site | date= 6 April 2016 | access-date= 14 April 2016 | archive-date= 11 October 2016 | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20161011205659/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/news-en/we-are-grateful-to-angela-merkel-for-her-leadership-in-the-ukraine-peace-process/ | url-status= dead }} 15 May 2016: Faction "Fatherland" preparing a statement in the name of the new Prosecutor General in connection with offenses in the activities of the National Commission, which performs state regulation in the energy and utilities relative to the unjustified increase of gas prices for the population.{{Cite web|url=https://ba.org.ua/batkivshhina-gotuye-zayavu-v-gpu-shhodo-neob%d2%91runtovanogo-zavishhennya-cini-na-gaz-dlya-naselennya-z-boku-nkrye/|title="Батьківщина" готує заяву в ГПУ щодо необґрунтованого завищення ціни на газ для населення з боку НКРЄ|date=14 May 2016|website=Батькiвщина}} 16 May 2016: Yulia Tymoshenko held a meeting with the coordinator of the US State Department on the issue of sanctions, Ambassador [[Dan Fried]]. The sides discussed the situation in Eastern Ukraine and Crimea, focusing on the politics of U.S. sanctions against Russia. Dan Fried assured Tymoshenko of the support of the United States of America for Ukraine, and its territorial integrity and independence.{{Cite web|url=https://ba.org.ua/yulya-timoshenko-zustrilasya-z-koordinatorom-derzhdepu-ssha-z-pitan-sankcij-2/|title=Юлія Тимошенко зустрілася з координатором Держдепу США з питань санкцій|date=16 May 2016|website=Батькiвщина}} 23 May 2016: At the initiative of Yulia Tymoshenko All-Ukrainian Union "Fatherland" launched a website "Fair rates", the proceedings of which explain the need to establish adequate tariffs for gas for the population.{{Cite news|url=https://ba.org.ua/batkivshhina-zapustila-sajt-pro-spravedlivi-tarifi/|title="Батьківщина" запустила сайт "Справедливі тарифи"|date=23 May 2016|website=Батькiвщина}}{{Cite web|url=https://taryfy.net/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160630123834/https://taryfy.net/|url-status=usurped|title=สล็อต เว็บใหญ่ pg - เกมสล็อตออนไลน์ อันดับ 1|archivedate=30 June 2016|website=สล็อต เว็บใหญ่ pg - เกมสล็อตออนไลน์ อันดับ 1}} Tymoshenko is in favor of extending the moratorium on land sales and supporting farmers.{{Cite news|title="Fatherland" has achieved an extension of the moratorium on the sale of agricultural land|url=https://ba.org.ua/batkivshhina-domoglasya-prodovzhennya-moratoriyu-na-prodazh-silskogospodarskoї-zemli/|work=Fatherland|date=7 December 2017|access-date=24 April 2018|language=uk}} She considers negotiations in the format of the Budapest Memorandum to be an effective way to resolve the issue of the war in Donbass.{{Cite news |title=Yulia Tymoshenko: guarantee of peace return to Ukraine – implementation of the Budapest Memorandum |url=https://ba.org.ua/yuliya-timoshenko-ti-xto-sogodni-imituyut-borotbu-za-mir-absolyutno-niyaki-diplomati/ |work=Fatherland |date=13 April 2018 |access-date=24 April 2018 |language=uk}} ====New course of Ukraine==== The New Deal of Ukraine is an election program for the post of President of Ukraine, which was presented on 15 June 2018, by the leader of the "Fatherland" party, Yulia Tymoshenko. This program provides for amendments to the Constitution of Ukraine.{{Cite report |author= |format= |url= https://constitution.nku.com.ua/ |title= НОВАЯ НАРОДНАЯ КОНСТИТУЦИЯ |type= |orig-year= |agency= |edition= Новый курс Украины |location= |year= 2018 |publisher= |at= |volume= |issue= |number= |pages= |page= |series= |isbn= |issn= |doi= |bibcode= |arxiv= |pmid= |language= |quote= |access-date= 27 April 2021 |archive-date= 30 May 2022 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220530101256/http://constitution.nku.com.ua/ |url-status= dead}}{{Cite report |author= |format= |url= https://defence.nku.com.ua/ |title= НОВАЯ СТРАТЕГИЯ МИРА И БЕЗОПАСНОСТИ |type= |orig-year= |agency= |edition= Новый курс Украины |location= |year= 2018 |publisher= |at= |volume= |issue= |number= |pages= |page= |series= |isbn= |issn= |doi= |bibcode= |arxiv= |pmid= |language= |quote= |access-date= 27 April 2021 |archive-date= 9 June 2022 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220609161125/http://defence.nku.com.ua/ |url-status= dead}} The program contains four blocks: # New social contract # New economic course # New World Strategy # Ecosystem of human life ===2019 presidential election=== [[File:Зустріч Зеленського з керівництвом ВРУ, 1.jpg|thumb|Yulia Tymoshenko, 21 May 2019]] On 20 June 2018, Tymoshenko announced that she would take part in the [[2019 Ukrainian presidential election]].[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/513305.html Tymoshenko says she will run for president in 2019], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (20 June 2018) She was a heavy favorite in the polls until early 2019.{{Citation needed|date=November 2022}} Two weeks before the election, candidate [[Serhiy Taruta]] pledged his campaign-team would support Tymoshenko's campaign efforts (however, his name was not taken off the ballot, the deadline to withdraw having been exceeded).{{Cite web|url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/573095.html|title=Taruta announces support for Tymoshenko at presidential elections|website=[[Interfax-Ukraine]]|access-date=16 March 2019}}{{Cite web |trans-title=Another candidate appeared in favor of Hrytsenko |script-title=uk:Ще один кандидат знявся на користь Гриценка |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2019/03/7/7208642/ |access-date=2023-07-08 |website=[[Ukrainska Pravda]] |language=uk}} On 8 February 2019, a new presidential candidate was registered with the same surname and initials as Yulia Tymoshenko – the non-factional people's deputy Yury V. Tymoshenko.{{Cite web|title="Double" for Tymoshenko. 10 facts about Yulia Tymoshenko|url= https://espreso.tv/article/2019/02/13/prezydentski_vybory_10_faktiv_pro_yuriya_tymoshenka|website=Espreso.tv|access-date=13 February 2019|language=uk}}{{Cite web|title=Tymoshenko was expelled from the "Popular Front" because of plans to run for president|url=https://hromadske.ua/posts/deputata-timoshenka-viklyuchili-z-narodnogo-frontu-cherez-plani-balotuvatis-u-prezidenti|website=Hromadske.ua|date=17 January 2019 |access-date=17 January 2019|language=uk}} It was done with the intention that the voters, especially older people, would make a mistake on the ballot. Tymoshenko herself called it a "dirty" move by Poroshenko]{{Cite web|title= Tymoshenko claims that People's Deputy Yuriy Tymoshenko is a technical candidate from the government against her|url= https://ua.interfax.com.ua/news/political/564614.html|website= ua.interfax|accessdate=8 February 2019|language=uk}} For a long period before the start of the election campaign, Tymoshenko was the leader of opinion polls.{{Cite news|title="Surfing" on moods: why does Tymoshenko lead the presidential rating?|url=https://www.bbc.com/ukrainian/features-russian-46273245|newspaper=BBC News Україна|access-date=20 November 2018|language=uk}} The situation changed after the actor [[Volodymyr Zelenskyy]]'s New Year's announcement of his intention to run for president, after which it was Zelenskyy who began to gain a rating, beating Tymoshenko at the end of January 2019. Later, during the entire election campaign, Tymoshenko shared 2–3 positions in the rating together with the current President Petro Poroshenko.{{Cite web|title=Ukraine faces a choice. Which of the race leaders will advance to the second round|url=https://www.lrt.lt/ru/novosti/17/934605/ukraina-pered-vyborom-kto-iz-liderov-gonki-vyidet-vo-vtoroi-tur|website=lrt.lt|date=29 March 2019 |access-date=29 March 2019|language=ru}} The first round of the election was held on 31 March 2019. With 13.4% of the vote, Tymoshenko came in third behind Zelenskyy (30.2%) and the incumbent Poroshenko (15.9%).[https://web.archive.org/web/20220123040934/https://www.cvk.gov.ua/pls/vp2019/wp300pt001f01=719.html First round results of the 2019 Ukrainian presidential election], [[Central Election Commission of Ukraine]] While she conceded defeat, she also accused Poroshenko of manipulating the results.{{cite web|url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/tymoshenko-ukraine-election-1.5081156|title=Ukraine's Tymoshenko, out of presidential contention, accuses Poroshenko of cheating|work=CBC|date=2 April 2019|access-date=3 April 2013}} In the [[2019 Ukrainian parliamentary election|2019 parliamentary election]], she led the "Fatherland" list which came in third, with 8.18% of the vote and 26 seats in parliament, in ahead of [[European Solidarity]] list led by Poroshenko (8.10%).{{cite web|url=https://www.rferl.org/a/final-ukraine-election-results-confirm-zelenskiy-landslide/30076731.html|title=Final Ukraine Election Results Confirm Zelenskiy Landslide|date=26 July 2019|access-date=27 July 2019|publisher=[[Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty]]}} ===Local elections 2020=== According to the results of the CEC, Tymoshenko's party received 4093 deputy mandates (12.39%) and became one of the leading parties in local elections in Ukraine.[https://rus.lb.ua/news/2020/11/18/470958_tsik_nazvali_partiilideri.html/ CEC named the party leaders in the local elections in Ukraine], ''lb.ua'' (18 November 2020) ===Parliamentary and political activities (2019–2021)=== In the 2019 parliamentary election, she led the Batkivshchyna list which came in third, with 8.18% of the vote and 26 seats in parliament.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/ukraine-politics/tymoshenkos-batkivshchyna-party-moves-to-opposition-over-land-market-law.html|title=Tymoshenko's Batkivshchyna party moves to opposition over land market law |date=13 November 2019|website=KyivPost|access-date=24 November 2019 |last1=Hnatyuk |first1=Vyacheslav }} Tymoshenko became a member of the Parliamentary Committee on Social Policy and Protection of Veterans' Rights.{{Cite web|title= People's Deputies of Ukraine. Yulia Tymoshenko|url= https://itd.rada.gov.ua/mps/info/page/1792|website= Official web portal of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine|accessdate= 29 August 2019|language= uk|archive-date= 27 January 2021|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20210127201409/https://itd.rada.gov.ua/mps/info/page/1792|url-status= dead}} Tymoshenko's activities in this session of the Rada focussed on opposition to lifting the moratorium on land sales and pushing for government action to lower energy prices. At the start of the term, Tymoshenko's party supported a number of measures put forward by the ruling Servant of the People party. On the first day of the new session of the Rada, Tymoshenko's party supported the bill on lifting parliamentary immunity.{{Cite web|title= Rada removed immunity from people's deputies |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/news/2019/09/3/7225166/|website=[[pravda]]|access-date=7 March 2021}} On 3 September 2019, the Batkivshchyna party supported the referral to the Constitutional Court of a bill to reduce the number of deputies.{{Cite web|title= "Fatherland" advocates reducing the number of deputies and giving the people the right of legislative initiative|url= https://ba.org.ua/batkivshhina-vistupaye-za-zmenshennya-kilkosti-deputativ-i-nadannya-narodovi-prava-zakonodavchoї-iniciativi/|website=Fatherland|date= 3 September 2019|accessdate=3 September 2019|language=uk}} However, in November 2019, after the parliament passed a bill to lift the moratorium on land sales, Yulia Tymoshenko announced her transition to opposition to the ruling Servant of the People party.{{Cite web|title="Fatherland" Tymoshenko goes into opposition to Zelensky's party |url=https://www.dw.com/uk/a-51230243|website=DW|access-date=7 March 2021|language=uk}} On 18 November 2019, Tymoshenko appealed to the Constitutional Court to immediately consider the petition for the bill on the "land market".{{Cite web|title= Tymoshenko's Batkivshchyna party moves to opposition over land market law |url=https://www.kyivpost.com/ukraine-politics/tymoshenkos-batkivshchyna-party-moves-to-opposition-over-land-market-law.html|website= kyivpost |date=13 November 2019 |access-date=18 November 2019|language=en |last1=Hnatyuk |first1=Vyacheslav }} In December 2019, Tymoshenko united more than 40 political and public organizations that oppose the sale of land in the National Headquarters for the Protection of Native Land. On 15 December 2019, the National Headquarters approved demands to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that it be necessary to postpone the adoption of "land laws", extend the moratorium and announce a referendum. The National Corps also joined the all-Ukrainian protest action initiated by the National Headquarters for the Protection of the Motherland.{{Cite web|title= National HQ for protection of native land requires Zelensky to hold referendum|url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/628881.html|website= interfax|access-date=6 December 2019|language=en}}{{Cite web|title=Нацкорпус підтримає всеукраїнську акцію протесту, ініційовану Національним штабом захисту рідної землі|url=https://nationalcorps.org/oficijno-nackorpus-pidtrimae-vseukrainsku-akciyu-protestu-inicijovanu-nacionalnim-shtabom-zahistu-ridnoi-zemli/|website=Національний корпус|date=15 December 2019|accessdate=15 December 2019|language=uk|archive-date=11 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211111124012/https://nationalcorps.org/oficijno-nackorpus-pidtrimae-vseukrainsku-akciyu-protestu-inicijovanu-nacionalnim-shtabom-zahistu-ridnoi-zemli/|url-status=dead}} On 19 December 2019, Yulia Tymoshenko and Batkivshchyna deputies addressed the National Anti-Corruption Bureau of Ukraine with a statement pointing to conflicts of interest and corruption in the Parliamentary Committee on Agrarian Policy during the consideration of the law on land sales.{{Cite web|title=Batkivshchyna calls on NABU to investigate corruption in pushing for land sale law|url=https://ba.org.ua/batkivshhina-zaklikala-nabu-rozsliduvati-korupciyu-pri-proshtovxuvanni-zakonu-pro-rozprodazh-zemli/|website= Батькивщина|date=19 December 2019|access-date=19 December 2019|language=uk}} In November 2020, Tymoshenko's party supported the all-Ukrainian SaveFOP campaign by registering Bill 3853–2 to simplify the taxation system for small businesses. Tymoshenko signed a memorandum of cooperation with the public movement SaveFOP.{{Cite web|title= Акция SaveФОП: предприниматели Украины отступать не намерены|url=https://www.dw.com/ru/akcija-savefop-predprinimateli-ukrainy-otstupat-ne-namereny/a-55827961|website= DW|access-date=12 April 2020|language=ru}} On 11 January 2021, Tymoshenko called on the authorities to provide Ukrainians with gas at a price no higher than the purchase price. According to Tymoshenko, the price of gas should not exceed ₴3, in this regard, Batkivshchyna registered Bill No. 1177 in the Verkhovna Rada.{{Cite web|title= Люди не должны платить за газ больше, чем он обошелся госмонополии – Тимошенко|url=https://interfax.com.ua/news/political/715260.html|website=Интерфакс Украина|access-date=11 April 2019|language=ru}} On 27 January, Tymoshenko initiated a referendum on five issues: the supply of Ukrainian gas and nuclear electricity to the population with a 30% profitability; on the sale of agricultural land; on the sale of strategic property; the issue of legalization of cannabis; about the gambling business. At the same time, Zelenskyy criticized Tymoshenko's referendum, although he himself initiated a nationwide poll on 5 issues, as well as the strengthening of democracy in Ukraine.{{Cite web|title= Tymoshenko To Initiate Referendums On 5 Issues|url=https://ukranews.com/en/news/752521-tymoshenko-to-initiate-referendums-on-5-issues|website= ukranews|date=27 January 2021|access-date=27 January 2021|language=en}}{{Cite web|title=Як українці відповіли на "5 питань Зеленського" – "Рейтинг" |url= https://www.ukrinform.ua/rubric-polytics/3123616-ak-ukrainci-vidpovili-na-5-pitan-zelenskogo-rejting.html|website= Укрінформ|date= 25 October 2020|accessdate=25 October 2020|language=uk}} On 1 March, the Batkivshchyna party demanded that the Government stop importing electricity from Russia and Belarus to Ukraine and launch an investigation into the matter. According to Tymoshenko, the import of Belarusian and Russian electricity threatens the national security of the country.{{Cite web|title= Тимошенко потребовала от СБУ расследовать импорт электроэнергии из РФ|url=https://www.mk.ru/politics/2021/03/01/timoshenko-potrebovala-ot-sbu-rassledovat-import-elektroenergii-iz-rf.html|website= MK|date=March 2021 |access-date=1 March 2021|language=ru}} In June, Tymoshenko took part in a meeting of the All-Ukrainian People's Council on holding a referendum against the sale of agricultural land.{{Cite web|url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/economic/747803.html|title=Tymoshenko: Batkivschyna submiting all necessary documents for start of referendum on land sale to CEC|website=Interfax-Ukraine}} On 21 July, Tymoshenko announced that the Batkivshchyna party had drafted a new Constitution of Ukraine, which provided for the division of power into four branches of government: legislative, executive, judicial, and control.{{cite web|url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/756996.html|title=Tymoshenko: Batkivschyna develops draft Constitution with fourth control branch of power|website=Interfax-Ukraine|date=21 July 2021|access-date=1 August 2021}} In September, Tymoshenko called on the Verkhovna Rada to adopt bill No. 4680, which provides for the supply of domestically produced gas to Ukrainian citizens at a low price/{{cite web|url=https://interfax.com.ua/news/economic/766856.html|title="Батькивщина" требует от Рады принять два тарифных закона|publisher=Interfax.com.ua|date=9 September 2021}} On 28 September, Tymoshenko registered in the Verkhovna Rada a Draft Resolution "On the Creation of a Temporary Investigative Commission to Investigate the Activities of NJSC Naftogaz".{{cite web|url=https://glavcom.ua/economics/finances/pitannya-obruntovanosti-tarifiv-na-gaz-hochut-doruchiti-vivchati-parlamentskiy-slidchiy-komisiji-786867.html|title= "Зробимо це разом?" Тимошенко оголосила війну "Нафтогазу"|publisher= Glavcom.ua|date=28 September 2021}} On 23 October, Tymoshenko registered in the Verkhovna Rada a draft resolution "On urgent measures to overcome an emergency level crisis that has developed as a result of an increase in energy prices" and a draft law "On Amendments to the Tax Code of Ukraine on the introduction of a reduced value added tax rate on energy carriers and related services".{{cite web|url=https://news.obozrevatel.com/ukr/politics/vladi-slid-dosluhatisya-do-propozitsij-timoshenko-ekspert.htm|title=Владі слід дослухатися до пропозицій Тимошенко, – експерт|publisher=Obozrevatel.com|date=23 November 2021}} On 26 October, Tymoshenko handed over medical equipment for patients with coronavirus to the Oleksandrivska Clinical Hospital in [[Kyiv]].{{Cite web|url=https://apostrophe.ua/news/politics/government/2021-10-26/vsya-strana-dolzhna-stat-edinoj-komandoj-v-borbe-s-kovidom--yuliya-timoshenko/247747|title=The whole country must become a united team in the fight against COVID – Yulia Tymoshenko|date=26 October 2021|website=Apostrophe.ua|access-date=26 October 2021|language=uk}} ===Parliamentary and political activities (2022–present)=== On 27 January 2022, the Verkhovna Rada adopted a resolution initiated by Tymoshenko to establish a Temporary Commission of Inquiry to investigate possible corruption that caused significant losses to the revenue side of the state budget, in particular as a result of NJSC Naftogaz. Tymoshenko became a member of this VSC.{{Cite web|title=Парламент створив комісію для перевірки Нафтогазу через можливі втрати держбюджету. Деталі|url= https://biz.liga.net/ua/ekonomika/tek/novosti/parlament-sozdal-komissiyu-dlya-proverki-naftogaza-iz-za-vozmojnyh-poter-gosbyudjeta-detali|website= Liga.net|date= 27 January 2022|accessdate= 27 January 2022|language=uk}} On 20 June, the Batkivshchyna faction, led by Yulia Tymoshenko, announced its opposition the ratification of the [[Istanbul Convention]]. Tymoshenko believes that the Istanbul Convention is not included in the package of requirements of the EU for granting Ukraine a candidate. She noted that the Verkhovna Rada went against the will of Ukrainians, because such an important issue should be determined at a national referendum.{{cite web|url= https://glavcom.ua/country/politics/yak-frakciji-ta-grupi-golosuvali-za-ratifikaciyu-stambulskoji-konvenciji-854422.html |title= As factions and groups voted for the ratification of the Istanbul Convention|date= 20 June 2022|publisher= Glavcom.ua|access-date= 20 June 2022}}{{cite web|url= https://lb.ua/news/2022/06/20/520640_deputati_ratifikuvali_stambulsku.html|title= Deputy of Ukraine have ratified the Istanbul Convention to gain EU candidate status|date= 20 June 2022|publisher= Lb.ua|access-date=20 June 2022}} On 19 July, deputies of the Batkivshchyna faction prevented the theft of 264 billion hryvnias from the budget, which [[Naftogaz of Ukraine]] was supposed to spend on an opaque scheme for financing the purchase of natural gas for the next heating season.{{in lang|uk}} [https://zn.ua/ECONOMICS/u-timoshenko-zajavili-o-slomannoj-deputatami-hazovoj-afere-naftohaza.html У Тимошенко заявили о сломанной депутатами "газовой" афере "Нафтогаза"], [[Zn.ua]] 19 July 2022){{cite web|url=https://konkurent.ua/publication/100571/blin-nu-poyasnit-meni-timoshenko-u-radi-zirvalasya-cherez-gaz-video/|title= Damn, well, explain to me - Tymoshenko broke down in the Rada due to gas (video)|publisher=Konkurent.ua|access-date=20 July 2022}}{{cite web|url=https://censor.net/ua/news/3356065/naftogaz_vvodyt_v_omanu_kabmin_vr_i_mijnarodnyh_partneriv_prosyt_dvichi_vydilyty_150_mlrd_grn_na_zakupivlyu|title="Naftogaz" misleads the Cabinet of Ministers, the Verkhovna Rada and international partners: it asks twice to allocate UAH 150 billion for the purchase of gas, - Tymoshenko. DOCUMENT|work=Цензор.НЕТ |date=23 July 2022 |publisher=Censor.net|access-date=23 July 2022 |last1=Нет |first1=Цензор }} On 29 July during a working visit to [[Japan]], Tymoshenko discussed the situation in Ukraine with [[Tokyo]] Governor [[Yuriko Koike]].{{cite web|url= https://ba.org.ua/yuliya-timoshenko-zustrilasya-z-gubernatorkoyu-tokio/|title= Yulia Tymoshenko met with the governor of Tokyo|date= 29 July 2022|publisher= ba.org.ua|access-date=29 July 2022}} On the same day, Tymoshenko spoke at a conference in Tokyo and called for the creation of a new stable security order in the world.{{cite web|url= https://ba.org.ua/yuliya-timoshenko-zaklikala-stvoriti-novij-stabilnij-bezpekovij-poryadok-u-sviti/|title= Yulia Tymoshenko called for the creation of a new stable security order in the world|date= 29 July 2022|publisher= ba.org.ua|access-date=29 July 2022}} ====2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine==== In late February 2022, Yulia Tymoshenko urged NATO and the [[United Nations]] to protect Ukraine by closing its airspace and deploying peacekeeping forces to the country.{{cite web |title='Putin is ready for anything,' says Ukraine's ex-PM Yulia Tymoshenko |url=https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/the-interview/20220228-putin-is-ready-for-anything-says-ukraine-s-ex-pm-yulia-tymoshenko |website=France24 |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=7 April 2022}} She later stated that Putin aims to recreate the Soviet Union, posing a threat to Eastern Europe, and emphasized that Ukraine's NATO membership is crucial for its security.{{cite news |last1=Maximof |first1=Yeger |title=Former Ukrainian PM Yulia Tymoshenko: Putin On 'Historic Mission' To Recreate The Soviet Union |url=https://www.rferl.org/a/31782709.html |newspaper=Radiofreeeurope/Radioliberty |access-date=7 April 2022}} In early March, Tymoshenko took custody of the [[Okhmatdyt]] children's hospital, donating medicines and helping evacuate sick children from Kyiv. She also established the Center for Humanitarian Aid to assist vulnerable populations and the Ukrainian military.{{Cite web|url=https://www.rbc.ua/rus/news/komanda-batkivshchiny-beret-opeku-ohmatdet-1646159604.html|title=Команда "Батькивщины" берет под опеку Охматдет, - Тимошенко|website=РБК-Украина}}{{Cite web|url=https://interfax.com.ua/news/general/815627.html|title="Батькивщина" оказывает дипломатическую и гуманитарную помощь, поддержку военных – Тимошенко|website=Интерфакс-Украина}} Throughout 2022, Tymoshenko actively advocated for Ukraine's integration into the EU and NATO, participating in various international forums. She met with European leaders to discuss Ukraine's humanitarian situation and global food security and spoke out against peace negotiations with Russia without the full restoration of Ukraine's territorial integrity.{{Cite web|url=https://war.obozrevatel.com/tolko-razgrom-armii-rf-i-rezhima-putina-privedet-k-skorejshemu-prekrascheniyu-vojnyi-timoshenko.htm|title=Только разгром армии РФ и режима Путина приведет к скорейшему прекращению войны, – Тимошенко|first=Мария|last=Шевчук|date=1 June 2022|website=WAR OBOZREVATEL}} Tymoshenko's international engagements included a call to rebuild the global security system and counter Russian disinformation.{{Cite web|url=https://www.obozrevatel.com/ukr/politics-news/timoshenko-na-kruglomu-stoli-the-economist-zaklikala-perebuduvati-globalnu-sistemu-bezpeki.htm|title=Тимошенко на круглому столі The Economist закликала перебудувати глобальну систему безпеки|first=Марія|last=Шевчук|date=5 July 2022|website=OBOZREVATEL}}{{Cite web|url=https://ba.org.ua/yuliya-timoshenko-zustrilasya-z-vikonavchim-direktorom-amerikanskogo-yevrejskogo-komitetu/|title=Юлія Тимошенко зустрілася з Виконавчим директором Американського єврейського комітету|date=6 July 2022|website=Батькiвщина}} In 2023, Tymoshenko continued her humanitarian efforts, raising funds for military hospitals and supporting wounded Ukrainian soldiers.{{Cite web|url=https://ba.org.ua/yuliya-timoshenko-likuvannya-ta-reabilitaciya-ukra%D1%97nskix-vijskovix-mayut-buti-na-najvishhomu-rivni-batkivshhina-iniciyuye-specialnij-zakon/|title=Юлія Тимошенко: Лікування та реабілітація українських військових мають бути на найвищому рівні – "Батьківщина" ініціює спеціальний закон|date=2 May 2023|website=Батькiвщина}}{{Cite web|url=https://vsim.ua/tochka-zoru/yuliya-timoshenko-finansuvannya-likuvannya-ta-reabilitatsiyi-viyskovih-11801010.html|title=Юлія Тимошенко: Фінансування лікування та реабілітації військових має бути збільшено у 2,6 раза - vsim.ua|website=vsim.ua}} She also took part in international conferences, advocating for Ukraine's needs and criticizing Russia's aggression.{{Cite web|url=https://fr.ncr-iran.org/publications/rapports-speciaux/conference-a-paris-pour-annoncer-le-soutien-de-117-anciens-dirigeants-au-peuple-iranien-et-a-sa-resistance/|title=Conférence à Paris pour annoncer le soutien de 117 anciens dirigeants au peuple iranien et à sa Résistance|first=Mansoureh|last=Galestan|date=22 June 2023}} Her parliamentary activities included opposing legislation that would harm businesses and advocating for improved medical treatment and rehabilitation for soldiers.{{Cite web|url=https://glavcom.ua/country/politics/julija-timoshenko-pro-skandalnij-zakonoprojekt-8401-nashe-zavdannja-skasuvati-vbivchi-dlja-biznesu-normi-939341.html|title=Тимошенко про скандальний закон 8401: наше завдання – скасувати вбивчі для бізнесу норми|date=4 July 2023|website=ГЛАВКОМ}} In 2024, Tymoshenko faced increased pressure from Russia, being placed on its wanted list.{{cite web |title=Russia puts former Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Tymoshenko on its wanted list |url=https://apnews.com/article/russia-tymoshenko-wanted-list-ukraine-a222c691884ac1d0f4a999ca6baf2169 |website=Associated Press |language=en |date=8 June 2024}} Despite this, she continued her leadership in the Batkivshchyna party, marking its 25th anniversary by organizing humanitarian aid for the Ukrainian military.{{Cite web|url=https://tsn.ua/politika/timoshenko-i-batkivschina-vidpravili-na-front-25-gumanitarnih-misiy-prisvyatili-vazhliviy-dati-2619546.html|title=Тимошенко і "Батьківщина" відправили на фронт 25 гуманітарних місій – присвятили важливій даті|date=12 July 2024|website=ТСН.ua}} She also initiated a parliamentary investigation into corruption in Ukraine's energy sector and remained active in international diplomacy, addressing corruption and advocating for Ukraine's sovereignty.{{Cite web|url=https://odessamedia.net/news/batkivshchina-initsiyue-stvorennya-tsk-shchodo-koruptsiji-v-energetichnikh-kompaniyakh|title="Батьківщина" ініціює створення ТСК щодо корупції в енергетичних компаніях|website=Odessamedia}} On 19 June 2025, Yulia Tymoshenko headed the inter-factional association for the protection of the rights of veterans, combatants and their families.{{Cite news|url=https://focus.ua/uk/ukraine/711161-yuliya-timoshenko-stvorila-ta-ocholila-parlamentske-mizhfrakciyne-ob-yednannya-iz-zahistu-prav-veteraniv-uchasnikiv-boyovih-diy-ta-jihnih-rodin|script-title=uk:Юлія Тимошенко створила та очолила парламентське міжфракційне об'єднання із захисту прав ветеранів, учасників бойових дій та їхніх родин|date=19 June 2025|work=centernews.com.ua|access-date=19 June 2025|language=uk-UA}} On 19 August 2025, Yulia Tymoshenko took part in the Extraordinary EPP Summit, which was held with the participation of EU leaders who joined the negotiations in Washington.{{Cite news|url=https://www.centernews.com.ua/?p=498709|script-title=uk:Юлія Тимошенко взяла участь у Надзвичайному Саміті ЄНП, який проходив за участі лідерів ЄС, які долучилися до переговорів у Вашингтоні|date=19 August 2025|work=centernews.com.ua|access-date=19 August 2025|language=uk-UA}} Since September 2025, with the support of Yulia Tymoshenko, the Batkivshchyna party has launched a "hotline" to help veterans and military personnel.{{Cite news|url=https://prawwwda.com/news/31971-partiya-batkivshchina-vidkrila-garyachu-liniyu-dlya-veteraniv-vijni|script-title=uk:Партія «Батьківщина» відкрила гарячу лінію для ветеранів війни|date=11 September 2025|work=prawwwda.com|access-date=11 September 2025|language=uk-UA}} ==Political views== {{See also|Batkivshchyna|Yulia Tymoshenko Bloc}} Tymoshenko wants her country to become a member state of the EU, while also expressing concern about antagonizing Russia,[https://web.archive.org/web/20090520060131/https://www.foreignpolicy.com/story/cms.php?story_id=4811&page=1 Ukraine's Dangerous Game] by Federico Fubini, [[Foreign Policy (magazine)|Foreign Policy]]. [https://www.unian.info/world/101928-tymoshenko-ukraine-will-become-a-member-of-the-european-union.html Tymoshenko: Ukraine will become a member of the European Union], [[UNIAN]] (3 March 2008) saying: "I try to defend our interests so that we can find a balance in our relations both with the EU and Russia." Tymoshenko supports [[Ukraine–NATO relations|Ukraine joining NATO]], stating it would be "uncomfortable" for Ukraine to remain "in a void, outside all existing security systems". According to Tymoshenko, the question of Ukraine joining any system of collective security would "be resolved only by referendum".[https://web.archive.org/web/20110209055401/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/51597/ Tymoshenko sure Ukraine will join European Union], ''Kyiv Post'' (31 October 2009) Tymoshenko favours close [[Ukraine and the European Union|relations with the EU]], including the creation of a [[free trade area]] between Ukraine and the EU[https://www.kmu.gov.ua/control/en/publish/article?art_id=222866500 Yulia Tymoshenko hopes today's sitting of Ukraine-EU Cooperation Council to give powerful impetus for Ukraine's accedence to the EU], Personal web site of Yulia Tymoshenko (16 June 2009) and later a full [[EU membership]].[https://web.archive.org/web/20101122232836/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/56990/ Updated: Tymoshenko wants to take country into EU in 5 years], ''Kyiv Post'' (14 January 2010) According to Tymoshenko, "The European project has not been completed as yet. It has not been completed because there is no full-fledged participation of Ukraine."[https://www.unian.info/world/229293-tymoshenko-european-project-not-finished-because-ukraine-is-not-there.html Tymoshenko: European project not finished because Ukraine is not there], [[UNIAN]] (4 June 2009) She opposes foreign intervention in internal Ukrainian affairs: "Ukraine's realization of its sovereign rights, forming a modern political nation, cannot be considered as a policy aimed against anyone."[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/18600.html Ukraine will independently decide on its domestic, foreign policies, says Tymoshenko], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (14 August 2009) Tymoshenko does not want to expand the lease contract of the [[Russian Black Sea fleet]] in Ukraine, saying: "The [[Constitution of Ukraine]] quite clearly stipulates that foreign military bases cannot be deployed in Ukraine, and this constitutional clause is the fundamental basis of the state's security."[https://web.archive.org/web/20110124110210/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/57025/ Tymoshenko: Constitution is the main priority regarding deployment of Russian fleet in Ukraine], ''Kyiv Post'' (14 January 2010) She also believes in "building a genuine civil society" as the best way to help democracy. Tymoshenko regards Ukraine as a "unitary and indivisible state". Tymoshenko considers separatist attitudes in Ukraine unacceptable: "Love one another, from [[Donetsk]], Crimea, [[Luhansk]], Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, [[Lviv]], [[Ternopil]], [[Ivano-Frankivsk]], Kyiv and all the other corners of our native land."[https://web.archive.org/web/20100901141044/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/s9zx9mz3 Yulia Tymoshenko: we must realize that Ukraine is an indivisible state], Official website of Yulia Tymoshenko (24 August 2010) According to Tymoshenko, citizens in Russian-speaking Dnipropetrovsk already understood Ukrainian in [[Soviet times]] and that problems surrounding the [[Russian language in Ukraine]] were "exaggerated and don't exist".{{Cite web |title=Tea with the Financial Times: Yulia Tymoshenko |url=https://www.unian.info/society/138485-tea-with-the-financial-times-yulia-tymoshenko.html |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=www.unian.info |date=18 August 2008 |language=en}} Tymoshenko opposes [[Russian language in Ukraine#Russian language in Ukrainian politics|the introduction of Russian]] as a second [[official state language]].[https://web.archive.org/web/20110713175812/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/47230/ Ukrainian premier against granting national status to Russian language], ''Kyiv Post'' (20 August 2009) On 7 April 2014, she stated she supported the [[Legislation on languages in Ukraine|2012 language law]] which is aimed at giving Russian and other minority languages (if in a region the percentage of a [[Demographics of Ukraine#After WWII|national minority]] exceeded 10%) the status of [[regional language]].[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/199537.html Russian language must retain regional language status – Tymoshenko], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (7 April 2014)[https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/lytvyn-program-for-development-of-ukrainian-language-should-be-adopted-before-elections-312012.html Lytvyn:Program for development of Ukrainian language should be adopted before elections], [[Kyiv Post]] (24 August 2012)[https://www.theguardian.com/world/2012/jul/04/ukrainians-protest-russian-language-law Ukrainians protest against Russian language law], [[The Guardian]] (4 July 2012) About her own attitude toward the Ukrainian language, Tymoshenko has stated that "today I am thinking in Ukrainian... and the fact that I know Russian very well, I think it is not a secret for you... you all know that I was brought up in the Russian speaking region in Dnipropetrovsk, to my mind, I spared no effort to speak Ukrainian as soon as possible as I came in the Government."[https://www.kmu.gov.ua/control/en/publish/article?art_id=160667097&cat_id=156277122 Yulia Tymoshenko: In Ukraine there will be "mova" (Ukrainian language), not "yazyk" (Russian language)!], Web portal of the Ukrainian Government (25 September 2008) Tymoshenko wrote an article called "Containing Russia" that was published in the May–June 2007 edition of the journal ''[[Foreign Affairs]]''.Y. Tymoshenko, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20070517113238/https://www.foreignaffairs.org/20070501faessay86307/yuliya-tymoshenko/containing-russia.html Containing Russia]" in ''[[Foreign Affairs]]'', May–June 2007, pp. 69–83.I. Khrestin, "[https://www.weeklystandard.com/article/19281 The Kremlin's Issue with Foreign Affairs] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131062149/http://www.weeklystandard.com/article/19281 |date=31 January 2016}}" in ''[[The Weekly Standard]]'', 17 April 2007. In the article she criticized Russian expansionism. Consequently, the article irked Russia and more than a week after the article was published, Russia responded by calling it an "anti-Russian manifesto" and "an attempt to once again draw dividing lines in Europe."Russian Embassy to South Africa, ''[https://web.archive.org/web/20081231020819/https://www.russianembassy.org.za/statements/text/apr07/Tymoshenko.html Russian MFA Information and Press Department Commentary Regarding a Question from RIA Novosti Concerning Yulia Tymoshenko's Article 'Containing Russia' in the Journal Foreign Affairs.]''{{#tag:ref|It was subsequently revealed that significant portions of the article were paraphrased from an article written by former U.S. Secretary of State [[Henry Kissinger]]. Tymoshenko's staff denied allegations of plagiarism on the grounds that the ''Foreign Affairs'' format does not usually include attributions.Editor's note, Foreign Affairs Vol 86 Issue 5 (September–October 2007) p. 195 Russian Foreign Minister [[Sergei Lavrov]] wrote an article called "Containing Russia: Back To The Future?"{{cite web|url=https://archive.mid.ru//brp_4.nsf/e78a48070f128a7b43256999005bcbb3/8f8005f0c5ca3710c325731d0022e227?OpenDocument |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160109180125/https://archive.mid.ru//brp_4.nsf/e78a48070f128a7b43256999005bcbb3/8f8005f0c5ca3710c325731d0022e227?OpenDocument |url-status=dead |archive-date=9 January 2016 |title=The Article by Russian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sergey Lavrov "Containing Russia: Back to the Future?" |language=ru|publisher=Mid.ru |date=19 July 2007 |access-date=18 February 2011}} for the same journal which was apparently meant to be a response to Tymoshenko. He withdrew the article before publication, accusing the editors of changing his text and said his article was subjected to "censorship".[https://www.rferl.org/featuresarticle/2007/07/76f94faf-fb05-4719-a104-97e2ddee97e5.html RFERL].|group=nb}} The first Tymoshenko Government was in favor of transparent and honest re-privatization of 3,000 enterprises,[https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/business/4270781.stm Ukraine revisits state sell-offs], BBC News (16 February 2005) as with the case of the Kyvorizhstal steel mill.{{cite news |last=Myers |first=Steven Lee |title=Ukraine Leader Fires Cabinet as Reform Coalition Splits |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2005/09/09/international/europe/09ukraine.html |newspaper=The New York Times International |date=9 September 2005 |access-date=8 October 2013}} Tymoshenko believes that [[Economy of Ukraine|Ukraine's economy]] is excessively monopolized.{{cite news|last=Slivka|first=Andrey|title=Bitter Orange|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2006/01/01/magazine/01tymoshenko.html?pagewanted=5&_r=1|access-date=29 July 2012|work=[[The New York Times Magazine]]|date=1 January 2006}}[https://web.archive.org/web/20091010171051/https://www.kyivpost.com/nation/50233 Tymoshenko: Ukraine's economy excessively monopolized], ''Kyiv Post'' (7 October 2009) Tymoshenko is against [[privatization]] of the [[Transport in Ukraine#Pipelines|gas transportation system in Ukraine]].[https://web.archive.org/web/20110209060959/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/52893/ Tymoshenko promises not to allow privatization of Ukraine's gas transportation system], ''Kyiv Post'' (16 November 2009) Tymoshenko lists the salvation of the economy of Ukraine during the [[2008–2009 Ukrainian financial crisis]] as one of her achievements.[https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/page/achievements Achievements], Official website of Yulia Tymoshenko {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151003144124/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/page/achievements |date=3 October 2015}} The second Tymoshenko Government has spent 1.6 billion [[hryvnya]] on modernizing the [[Coal mining in Ukraine|coal mining]] industry.[https://www.kmu.gov.ua/control/en/publish/article?art_id=237394521&cat_id=156277122 Yulia Tymoshenko: Effective development of coal industry is the future of Ukraine], [[Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine]] (25 August 2009) Tymoshenko wants to raise the general level of social standards by equalizing salaries in the industrial and social spheres,[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/21272.html Government plans raise general level of social standards, says Tymoshenko], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (1 October 2009) and pledged in November 2009 to revamp [[Health in Ukraine|Ukraine's hospitals and health system]] within two years.[https://web.archive.org/web/20110217050327/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/51620/ Tymoshenko pledges to revamp ailing hospitals by 2012], ''Kyiv Post'' (1 November 2009) She also pledged tax breaks for farmers.[https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/economic/26067.html Tymoshenko promises tax breaks for farmers], [[Interfax-Ukraine]] (25 November 2009) Other economic policies included compensation for depositors who lost Soviet-era savings, price controls on food and medicines to bring inflation down, and calls for a review of murky privatizations and high social spending.[https://web.archive.org/web/20100117194308/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/56302/ Ukraine's election: portraits of main players], ''Kyiv Post'' (1 January 2010) Tymoshenko wants to cut the number of taxes by a third to simplify the system, and wants to cut the [[Value Added Tax]] (VAT) and offer tax breaks to importers of new technologies to poor regions to boost investment there. In December 2009, the second Tymoshenko Government proposed creating independent anti-corruption bureaus in Ukraine.[https://web.archive.org/web/20091212093440/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/54563/ Tymoshenko proposes creating independent anti-corruption bureaus in Ukraine], ''Kyiv Post'' (8 December 2009) Tymoshenko believes Ukraine can gain energy security and independence, and she wants to speed up exploration and extraction of oil and [[natural gas in Ukraine|natural gas]] from the [[Black Sea shelf]].[https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/economic-policies-of-ukraines-election-front-runne-57418.html Economic policies of Ukraine's election front-runners], ''Kyiv Post'' (18 January 2010) Considering [[Nuclear power in Ukraine|nuclear power]] provides almost 50% of the electricity supply in Ukraine, Tymoshenko's government agreed to cooperate with the company Westin to establish factory production of nuclear fuel in Ukraine, independent of Russia. She also suggested a 10-year tax break for enterprises that would develop alternative energy sources in Ukraine.{{citation needed|date=February 2022}} Tymoshenko is for the cancellation of Verkhovna Rada deputies' [[Ukrainian deputy|immunity from prosecution]].[https://web.archive.org/web/20100516055907/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66135/ Tymoshenko denies seeking deputy immunity from prosecution], ''Kyiv Post'' (8 May 2010) For Ukraine, Tymoshenko prefers the [[proportional representation]] [[voting system]] with [[open list]]s.[https://web.archive.org/web/20121006002141/https://www.kyivpost.com/content/politics/tymoshenko-proportional-voting-system-is-the-best--67699.html Tymoshenko: Proportional voting system is the best for Ukraine], ''Kyiv Post'' (26 May 2010) Tymoshenko wants to reform the forming of state executive bodies,[https://www.unian.info/politics/279740-tymoshenko-proposes-to-change-staff-policy-in-country-cardinally.html Tymoshenko proposes to change staff policy in country cardinally], [[UNIAN]] (22 October 2009) and favours giving parliamentary opposition "real instruments of influence on the [[Government of Ukraine|authorities]]". She also wants [[Law of Ukraine#Criminal justice system|Ukrainian court system]] reforms[https://web.archive.org/web/20110713175902/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/50107/ Tymoshenko promises to establish 'dictatorship of law' if she wins at presidential elections], ''Kyiv Post'' (5 October 2009) and wants devolution of executive power to [[local authorities]].[https://web.archive.org/web/20110713175935/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/50097/ Tymoshenko speaks in support of decentralization of power], ''Kyiv Post'' (5 October 2009)[https://web.archive.org/web/20110713180052/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/50391/ Tymoshenko approves of Poroshenko as foreign minister], ''Kyiv Post'' (9 October 2009) Together with representatives of regional governments, Tymoshenko expanded a Law that aimed to empower local authorities. In the summer of 2009, she claimed she tried to bring together different political parties in order to amend the constitution and switch to a [[parliamentary form of government]]. In February 2011, Tymoshenko stated "Viktor Yanukovych's naked attempt to hijack the election that precipitated the Orange Revolution should have resulted in him being banned from running in future elections." In November 2009, Tymoshenko called Ukraine "an absolutely ungovernable country" due to the changes to the Constitution of Ukraine as a part of a political compromise between the acting authorities (former-President Kuchma) and opposition during the Orange Revolution.[https://web.archive.org/web/20091130180555/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/53612 Tymoshenko calls Ukraine 'absolutely ungovernable'], ''Kyiv Post'' (26 November 2009) Tymoshenko has characterised those reforms as "incomplete",{{cite book|title=The Report: Emerging Ukraine 2007|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ATsTI1GeTs8C&pg=PA20|access-date=30 January 2016|year=2007|publisher=Oxford Business Group|isbn=978-1-902339-68-9|page=20}} and the Yulia Tymoshenko Bloc voted against them in December 2004.[https://www.taraskuzio.net/media18_files/46.pdf Ukraine's constitutional crisis drags on] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724092950/https://www.taraskuzio.net/media18_files/46.pdf |date=24 July 2011}} by [[Taras Kuzio]], ''Kyiv Post'' (10 January 2007) In January 2010, Tymoshenko called for urgent amendments to the Constitution via the majority of the Verkhovna Rada after a survey or plebiscite is conducted.[https://web.archive.org/web/20100127161838/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/57605/ Tymoshenko: Constitution must be amended after survey conducted on form of government], ''Kyiv Post'' (20 January 2010) In April 2011, she still believed the constitution "didn't work".[https://www.unian.info/politics/484388-tymoshenko-idea-of-united-opposition-is-great-deception-by-yanukovych.html Tymoshenko: idea of united opposition is great deception by Yanukovych], [[UNIAN]] (15 April 2010) On 21 May 2016, Tymoshenko expressed hope that the EU will provide Ukraine a visa-free regime. Tymoshenko stressed that the Ukrainian state is there to protect the world all over Europe, continuing to fight the Russian aggression.[https://uapress.info/uk/news/show/131743 The Ukrainian people deserve to receive a visa-free regime – Tymoshenko], uapress.info (21 May 2016) ==Electoral history== ===Presidential elections=== {| class="wikitable" ! rowspan="2"|Election ! rowspan="2"|Party ! colspan=3|First round result ! colspan=3|Second round result |- !Votes !% !Result !Votes !% !Result |- | [[2019 Ukrainian presidential election|2019]]{{Cite news|url=https://www.rferl.org/a/final-results-from-ukraine-presidential-election-give-zelenskiy-30-percent-of-vote/29860319.html|title=Final Ukraine Results Give Zelenskiy 30 Percent Of Presidential Vote|newspaper=Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty|date=4 April 2019 |language=en|access-date=21 April 2019}} | [[All-Ukrainian Union "Fatherland"|Fatherland]] | 2,532,452 | 13.40% | {{no|Third}} | {{n/a}} | {{n/a}} | {{n/a}} |- | [[2014 Ukrainian presidential election|2014]]{{cite web |url=https://www.nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/566632/ |title=Poroshenko wins presidential election with 54.7% of vote – CEC |publisher=[[Radio Ukraine International]] |date=29 May 2014 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140529212731/https://www.nrcu.gov.ua/en/148/566632/ |archive-date=29 May 2014}}
{{in lang|ru}} [https://telegraf.com.ua/ukraina/politika/1300294-rezultatyi-vyiborov-prezidenta-ukrainyi-2014-tsik-obrabotala-51-99.html Results election of Ukrainian president], Телеграф (29 May 2014) | [[All-Ukrainian Union "Fatherland"|Fatherland]] | 2,310,050 | 12.81% | {{no|Second}} | {{n/a}} | {{n/a}} | {{n/a}} |- | [[2010 Ukrainian presidential election|2010]]{{in lang|uk}} [https://gazeta.ua/index.php?id=324402 ЦВК оприлюднила офіційні результати 1-го туру виборів], Gazeta.ua (25 January 2010){{Cite web |url=https://www.sras.org/russian_journalism_feb_2010 |title=Yulia Timoshenko received 45.47 percent, or 11.6 million votes |access-date=11 March 2014 |archive-date=30 November 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101130035316/https://sras.org/russian_journalism_feb_2010 |url-status=dead}} | [[All-Ukrainian Union "Fatherland"|Fatherland]] | 6,159,810 | 25.05% | {{depends|Second}} | 11,593,357 | 45.47% | {{no|Lost}} |- |} ===Parliamentary elections=== {{Expand section|date=April 2020}} ==Family and personal life== {{Main|Family of Yulia Tymoshenko}} Yulia Tymoshenko's mother, Lyudmila Mykolayivna Telehina (born Nelepova), was born on 11 August 1937 in [[Dnipropetrovsk]]. Her father, Volodymyr Abramovych Hrihyan, was born on 3 December 1937, also in Dnipropetrovsk. His Soviet passport gave his nationality as Latvian. His mother was Maria Yosypivna Hrihyan, born in 1909. [[Ukrainian media]] have published speculation regarding the genealogy of Tymoshenko. Some of the hypotheses have no scientific evidence (for example, the hypothesis of the [[Armenian people|Armenian]] origin of the surname "Grigyan"); {{Cite web |title=Тимошенко - україно-латишка, але любить євреїв |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2005/09/2/3012934/ |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=Українська правда |language=uk}}{{Cite news |title=Debate Rages Over Whether Ukraine Presidential Hopeful Is Jewish |language=en |work=Haaretz |url=https://www.haaretz.com/2009-10-11/ty-article/debate-rages-over-whether-ukraine-presidential-hopeful-is-jewish/0000017f-e0b0-d7b2-a77f-e3b725280000 |access-date=2023-04-18}}{{Cite web |date=2010-02-08 |title=Kyiv Post. Independence. Community. Trust - Nation - Campaign gets dirty: Leaflets smear Tymoshenko as 'Jew' |url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/58739/ |access-date=2023-04-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100208160009/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/58739/ |archive-date=8 February 2010}} or could be designed to create negative [[public relations|publicity]],{{Cite web |title=Война информационная, священная война... |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/rus/articles/2008/10/16/4452863/ |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=Украинская правда |language=uk}}[https://ru.tsn.ua/ukrayina/v-snyatine-ustanovyat-memorialnuyu-dosku-abramu-kapitelmanu-dedu-timoshenko.html In Sniatyn install a plaque Abram Kapitelmanu – grandfather Tymoshenko.] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181104170126/https://ru.tsn.ua/ukrayina/v-snyatine-ustanovyat-memorialnuyu-dosku-abramu-kapitelmanu-dedu-timoshenko.html |date=4 November 2018}}, ru.tsn.ua, 2 September 2009. – Provocative initiative "Party of Regions" (the author is a former member of "the Yulia Tymoshenko Bloc" Zinovy Boychuk) – under the advocacy company of "Tymoshenko is Jewish woman". although her Minister of Communications had in 2005 described her origins as half-Jewish, half-Armenian.{{Cite web |title=Рабинович призывает не спекулировать на избиении студента-еврея в Киеве |url=https://korrespondent.net/ukraine/events/129586-rabinovich-prizyvaet-ne-spekulirovat-na-izbienii-studenta-evreya-v-kieve |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=korrespondent.net |language=ru}} About her ethnicity, Yulia Tymoshenko herself has said: "On my father's side – everyone is Latvian for ten generations, and on my mother's side – everyone is Ukrainian for ten generations."{{cite web|url=https://farysei.narod.ru/makuha/book2.html |title=# b2_2 The reply of the Prime Minister of Ukraine Yulia Tymoshenko to blame the Minister of Transport "Chervonenko, Eugene Alfredovich", autumn 2005 |publisher=Farysei.narod.ru |access-date=11 October 2011}} Tymoshenko's parents were both born in Ukraine and are, therefore, Ukrainian as defined by the [[Law on Citizenship of Ukraine]] and by the [[Ukrainian Constitution]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mfa.gov.ua/mfa/en/273.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081113161029/https://www.mfa.gov.ua/mfa/en/273.htm|archive-date=13 November 2008|title=Citizenship|access-date=18 April 2008|work=Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine}}{{cite web|url=https://www.mfa.gov.ua/mfa/en/publication/content/719.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081204201403/https://www.mfa.gov.ua/mfa/en/publication/content/719.htm|archive-date=4 December 2008|title=Law on Citizenship of Ukraine|access-date=18 April 2008|work=[[Verkhovna Rada|Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine]]|publisher=Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine}}{{Cite web |date=2010-12-18 |title=Kyiv Post. Independence. Community. Trust - Opinion - OP-ED - Gender bias, anti-Semitism contributed to Yanukovych's victory |url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/opinion/op_ed/detail/62040 |access-date=2023-04-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101218222442/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/opinion/op_ed/detail/62040 |archive-date=18 December 2010}} Tymoshenko has said that, like most Soviet citizens, she spoke only Russian in her childhood (although she studied the Ukrainian language and [[Ukrainian literature|literature]] at school for ten years, as did all schoolchildren in Soviet Ukraine). In January 2010, Tymoshenko stated that in Dnipropetrovsk she did not have to speak Ukrainian until she was 36 (i.e. before 1996).{{Cite web |last=Новости |first=Р. И. А. |date=2010-01-03 |title=Тимошенко признала, что украинский язык выучила в 36 лет |url=https://ria.ru/20100103/202779743.html |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=РИА Новости |language=ru |quote={{Langx|ru|Я начала говорить на украинском, когда мне было где-то 36 лет, а до этого у меня мама и вся семья – мы все разговаривали на русском. У меня до сих пор мама обычно не разговаривает на украинском.}} (I began to speak in Ukrainian when I was around 36. Before that, my mother and the rest of my family, we all conversed in Russian. My mother still usually doesn't talk in Ukrainian.)}} According to Tymoshenko, her [[Braid (hairstyle)|braids]] are a family tradition. In 1979, Yulia married businessman Oleksandr Tymoshenko{{cite web|url= https://file.liga.net/person/320-uliya-timoshenko.html |title= Dossier: Yulia Tymoshenko |publisher=File.liga.net |date=27 November 1960 |access-date=11 October 2011}} (born 11 June 1960). The couple have a daughter – [[Eugenia Tymoshenko|Yevhenia (Eugenia) Tymoshenko]][https://www.theguardian.com/world/2011/oct/16/yulia-tymoshenko-daughter-yevhenia-carr?INTCMP=SRCH Yulia Tymoshenko's daughter: 'My mother's trial was a trial of revenge'], ''[[The Guardian]]'' (16 October 2011)
[https://www.theguardian.com/world/2012/sep/23/eugenia-tymoshenko-fight-to-save-mother-yulia Eugenia Tymoshenko: the fight to save my mother Yulia], ''[[The Guardian]]'' (23 September 2012){{Cite web |title=Донька Тимошенко взяла мамине прізвище. Для політики? |url=https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2011/12/26/6867849/ |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=Українська правда |language=uk}} (born 20 February 1980) – a graduate of the [[London School of Economics]] (Bsc "Government", Msc "Russian and Post-Soviet Studies"). ===Personal life=== Tymoshenko and her husband rent a house in Kyiv and own an apartment in Dnipro. Houses in Dnipro belong to their relatives.{{Cite web |title=Tymoshenko declares her incomes |url=https://www.unian.info/society/108197-tymoshenko-declares-her-incomes.html |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=www.unian.info |date=7 April 2008 |language=en}} Tymoshenko has declared she never used and will never use or move into a state-owned [[dacha|summer house]],{{Cite web |date=2011-10-09 |title=As President, Yulia Tymoshenko will not use state dachas - Yulia Tymoshenko. Official Website. |url=https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/c2nxnnv8 |access-date=2023-04-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111009164610/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/c2nxnnv8 |archive-date=9 October 2011}}{{Cite web |date=2012-03-14 |title=Yulia Tymoshenko has never used a government summer house - Yulia Tymoshenko. Official Website. |url=https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/mx56a7ss |access-date=2023-04-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120314222109/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/mx56a7ss |archive-date=14 March 2012}} in contrast with all former-Presidents and many high-ranking officials of Ukraine, who live in state-owned dachas in [[Koncha-Zaspa]].{{Cite web |last=Tuchynska |first=Svitlana |date=2010-04-29 |title=Ukrainska Pravda exposes how Yanukovych acquired Mezhygirya mansion - Apr. 29, 2010 |url=https://archive.kyivpost.com/article/content/ukraine-politics/ukrayinska-pravda-exposes-how-yanukovych-acquired-65388.html |access-date=2023-07-08 |website=Kyiv Post}} According to Ukrainian media, Tymoshenko lives in an estate in Koncha-Zaspa, "rented from a friend". In March 2014, Tymoshenko opened the door of her house to public activists and guided them around.{{cite news|url= https://tabloid.pravda.com.ua/photos/5333f5829cc40/|script-title= uk:Тимошенко на підборах показала активістам свій маєток у Конча-Заспі|trans-title=Tymoshenko showed activists around her mansion in Koncha Zaspa in her high heels|language=uk|publisher= Tabloid.pravda.com.ua|date= 27 March 2014|access-date= 26 July 2014|work= ТаблоID}} In her spare time, before she was imprisoned, Tymoshenko ran on a [[treadmill]] for exercise and listened to the music of [[Andrea Bocelli]], [[Sarah Brightman]], [[Anna Netrebko]] and [[Alessandro Safina]]. ''Ukrainska Pravda'' and ''[[LB.ua|Left Bank]]'' are her favourite news sources. {{Cite web |title=Тимошенко у домогосподарки не піде, а у Facebook - обіцяє |url=https://tabloid.pravda.com.ua/person/4d4a92dc5f4cb/ |access-date=2023-04-18 |website=ТаблоID}} {{request quotation|date=February 2016}} Tymoshenko stated that she watched the [[Tunisian Revolution]] and [[Egyptian Revolution of 2011]] "with joy and admiration". On 23 August 2020, Tymoshenko tested positive for [[COVID-19]] and was hospitalized in serious condition, with one of her spokespeople saying that "her condition is assessed as serious, her temperature is up to 39 [Celsius ({{convert|39|C|F|disp=out}})]".{{cite news |last1=Tiwari |first1=Pushkar |title=Former Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Tymoshenko in critical condition after testing coronavirus positive |url=https://zeenews.india.com/world/former-ukrainian-prime-minister-yulia-tymoshenko-in-critical-condition-after-testing-coronavirus-positive-2304820.html |access-date=23 August 2020 |work=[[Zee News]] |date=23 August 2020}} On 25 August, Tymoshenko was moved to the [[intensive care unit]] after her health worsened and prompted a transfer, a spokeswoman said, adding that she remains in "serious condition."{{cite news |last1=Verbyany |first1=Volodymyr |title=Ex-Ukrainian PM Tymoshenko Moved to Intensive Care With Covid-19 |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2020-08-25/ex-ukrainian-pm-tymoshenko-moved-to-intensive-care-with-covid-19 |access-date=26 August 2020 |work=[[Bloomberg L.P.|Bloomberg]] |date=25 August 2020}} On 2 September, Tymoshenko announced through her [[Facebook]] account that "Finally today, my crisis condition is behind. And although recovery is still a distant prospect, now there is an opportunity to return to normal life, step by step," adding "that fighting off a serious disease for almost two weeks alters the perception of reality".{{cite web|url=https://www.unian.info/politics/tymoshenko-ill-on-covid-19-mp-speaks-11132585.html|title=Tymoshenko speaks up first time after testing COVID-19 positive|date=2 September 2020|publisher=[[Ukrainian Independent Information Agency]]|access-date=5 September 2020}} On 11 September Tymoshenko's press secretary Maryna Soroka announced that Tymoshenko had tested negative for COVID-19.{{cite web|url=https://www.unian.info/society/covid-19-tymoshenko-recovers-from-coronavirus-11143268.html|title=Tymoshenko recovers from coronavirus – press secretary|date=11 September 2020|publisher=[[Ukrainian Independent Information Agency]]|access-date=11 September 2020}} ==Cultural and political image== Tymoshenko is a voluble public performer.[https://web.archive.org/web/20110128152034/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/58428/ Ukraine's Yanukovich shuns TV face-off with rival], ''Kyiv Post'' (1 February 2010) Her fiery rhetoric made her an icon of the Orange Revolution. Tymoshenko's critics have suggested that, as an oligarch, she gained her fortune improperly. Her former business partner, former Ukrainian prime minister Lazarenko, was convicted in the United States on charges of [[money laundering]], [[corporate crime|corruption]] and fraud, the magnitude of which was in the billions of dollars.{{cite news|title=Former Ukraine PM is jailed in US|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/americas/5287870.stm|work=BBC News |date=25 August 2006|access-date=26 August 2006}} However, on 7 May 2004, Judge Martin Jenkins of the US District Court for the Northern District of California dismissed the allegations of Tymoshenko's involvement in Lazarenko's murky business.{{cite web|last=Kupchinsky |first=Roman |url=https://www.rferl.org/content/article/1052721.html |title=Many Charges Dropped Against Ukraine's Ex-Premier |publisher=Rferl.org |date=10 May 2004 |access-date=28 December 2013}} Her transition from [[Ukrainian oligarch|oligarch]] to reformer was believed by many voters to be both genuine and effective.{{cite news|last=Levy|first=Clifford J.|title='The braid' is crowning glory for the Ukrainian politician Yulia Tymoshenko|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2007/10/07/world/europe/07iht-hair.4.7786675.html|access-date=29 July 2012|date=7 October 2007|newspaper=The New York Times}} Discrepancies between her declared income and her luxurious designer outfits, have been pointed out in the Ukrainian tabloids.{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/70065/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100623011139/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/70065/|archive-date=23 June 2010|title=While leading lavish lifestyles, politicians declare almost pauper-like incomes, assets|publisher=Kyiv Post|date=17 June 2010}}{{in lang|uk}} [https://tabloid.pravda.com.ua/brand/4a391ace277c4/ Тимошенко і "Віттон" – Люксембурзький "мове тон"], [[Tablo ID]] (17 June 2009){{in lang|uk}} [https://tabloid.pravda.com.ua/brand/4922e4a28d23c/ Юлія Тимошенко і Катерина Ющенко вдарили по Chanel], Tablo ID (19 November 2008) When Tymoshenko joined the Yushchenko government, she did not speak Ukrainian. According to fellow Ukrainian politician Borys Tarasyuk, in 2002 Tymoshenko "only spoke Russian even when I spoke to her in Ukrainian", but since then she has made the transition to speaking only Ukrainian.{{cite book|first=Laada|last=Bilaniuk|title=Contested Tongues: Language Politics and Cultural Correction in Ukraine|url=https://archive.org/details/contestedtongues00bila|url-access=registration|access-date=30 January 2016|year=2005|publisher=Cornell University Press|isbn=0-8014-7279-2|pages=[https://archive.org/details/contestedtongues00bila/page/68 68]–69}}{{cite news|url=https://www.boston.com/news/world/europe/articles/2010/01/13/orange_leaders_face_shutout_in_ukraine_election/|title=Orange Leaders Face Shutout in Ukraine Election|work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]|date=13 January 2010|access-date=29 January 2016}} During her second stint as prime-minister her ratings in opinion polls fell. In early 2008, in opinion polls for the [[2009 Ukrainian presidential election]], she stood at 30% but by late April 2009 that had shrunk to 15%See [[2010 Ukrainian presidential election#Opinion polls]] for references and more information According to a poll carried out between 29 January and 5 February 2009 by the [[Kyiv International Institute of Sociology]], just over 43% of the Ukrainian voters believed Tymoshenko should leave her post, whereas just over 45% believed she should stay.{{cite news|url=https://www.unian.info/society/191796-poll-says-ukraines-president-should-step-down-now.html|title=Poll says Ukraine's president should step down now|work=UNIAN/RIA Novosti|date=17 February 2009|access-date=29 January 2016}} According to an opinion poll carried out between 3 and 12 February 2009 by the "Sofia" Center for Social Studies, some 59.1% of those polled believed that the activities of (then) Prime Minister Yulia Tymoshenko were aimed at the defense of her own interests and that of her entourage, some 4.2% said her activities were aimed at the defense of interests of foreign states, and some 23.9% believed that Tymoshenko worked for the sake of national interests. 77.7% of the respondents were unsatisfied with the economic policy of the second Tymoshenko Government. Some 71.8% believed that this government was not able to lead the Ukrainian economy out of the [[2008–2009 Ukrainian financial crisis]] or even change the situation in Ukraine to better; 18.1% of respondents did think that the government could do that.{{cite news|url=https://www.unian.info/politics/191826-majority-of-ukrainians-believe-tymoshenko-defends-her-own-interests.html|title=Majority of Ukrainians believe Tymoshenko defends her own interests|work=UNIAN|date=17 February 2009|access-date=7 November 2015}}{{cite news|url=https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/8403.html|title=Ukrainians have lost confidence in government's handling of crisis, says poll|agency=Interfax-Ukraine|date=17 February 2009|access-date=7 November 2015}} Despite the neck-to-neck 2010 presidential race, many experts believed that Tymoshenko would win the vote due to her ability to "hike her popularity just before the voting day". JP Morgan Securities Inc. experts said that Tymoshenko's victory in presidential election would "bring stability in 2010, with budget consolidation, better terms of crediting and higher influx of capital. As a result, the economy will have better prospects of growing in the second half of 2010 and 2011".{{cite web|url=https://zik.ua/en/news/2009/10/31/202645|title=What's in store for Ukraine if Tymoshenko wins presidential election|publisher=Zik.ua|date=31 October 2009|access-date=28 December 2013|archive-date=2 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131202232150/http://zik.ua/en/news/2009/10/31/202645|url-status=dead}} Tymoshenko has been ranked three times by ''Forbes'' magazine among the most powerful women in the world. During her first term in 2005, she was ranked third (behind Condoleezza Rice and Wu Yi), in 2008 she was number 17{{cite news|title=The 100 Most Powerful Women|url=https://www.forbes.com/lists/2008/11/biz_powerwomen08_The-100-Most-Powerful-Women_Rank.html|work=Forbes|date=27 August 2008|access-date=27 August 2008}} and in 2009 at number 47.{{cite news|title=The 100 Most Powerful Women – No.47 Yulia Tymoshenko|url=https://www.forbes.com/lists/2009/11/power-women-09_Yulia-Tymoshenko_PGEZ.html|work=Forbes|date=20 August 2009}} According to the Ukrainian magazine ''[[Focus (Ukrainian magazine)|Focus]]'', Tymoshenko placed first in an annual ranking of the most influential women in Ukraine in 2006–2010 (five years).{{cite news|url=https://tabloid.pravda.com.ua/focus/4ce52c33d597e/|script-title=uk:ТОП-100 найвпливовіших жінок: родички Кучми та Ющенка і "невмируща" Тимошенко|trans-title=Top 100 most influential women: relatives of Kuchma and Yushchenko and the "immortal" Tymoshenko|language=uk|publisher=Tabloid.pravda.com.ua|date=18 November 2010|access-date=7 November 2015 |work=ТаблоID }}{{cite web|url=https://focus.ua/ratings/156273/|script-title=ru:100 самых влиятельных женщин Украины. Рейтинг Фокуса|trans-title=The 100 most influential women in Ukraine. Focus's rating|language=ru|publisher=Focus.ua|date=18 November 2010|access-date=7 November 2015}} During the Orange Revolution, some Western media publications dubbed her the "[[Joan of Arc]] of the Revolution". In December 2011, Tymoshenko's party [[BYuT-Batkivschyna]] nominated her for the [[Nobel Peace Prize]].{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111230093249/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/119025/|archive-date=30 December 2011|title=Korolevska: BYT nominating Tymoshenko for Nobel Peace Prize|work=Kyiv Post|date=15 December 2011}} Russian prime minister Vladimir Putin has stated (in November 2009) he found it comfortable to work with his (then) Ukrainian counterpart Yulia Tymoshenko and also praised her for strengthening Ukrainian sovereignty and building stable ties with Moscow{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/53261/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110217043924/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/53261/|archive-date=17 February 2011|title=Russia's Putin says comfortable with Ukraine's Tymoshenko|work=Kyiv Post|date=20 November 2009}} and called the second Tymoshenko Government "efficient and a force for stability".{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/53265/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091124072235/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/53265/|archive-date=24 November 2009|title=Putin gives Ukraine a break on gas deal|work=Kyiv Post|date=20 November 2009}} It has been suggested by [[Reuters]] that the [[Russian government]], after seeing her opposition to Viktor Yushchenko, supported her since late 2008, although Putin denied it. Former ally and President of Ukraine Yushchenko stated in November 2009, "I am sure that every week spent by Yulia Tymoshenko in the post of prime minister leads the country to a catastrophe. Because of Yulia Tymoshenko, it is a crisis, a crisis in everything".{{cite news|url=https://www.unian.info/politics/292921-tymoshenkos-activity-at-post-of-prime-minister-is-reason-of-crises-in-ukraine-yushchenko.html|title=Tymoshenko's activity at post of Prime Minister is reason of crises in Ukraine – Yushchenko|work=UNIAN|date=24 November 2009|access-date=7 November 2015}} Yushchenko has repeatedly accused his former ally turned rival Tymoshenko of acting in the interests of Russia, although she firmly denied the allegations. On 31 May 2010, Yushchenko stated that Tymoshenko was his "worst mistake", "The most serious mistake was to give the power to her twice".[https://web.archive.org/web/20100604055659/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/68117 Yushchenko told about his 'worst mistake'], ''Kyiv Post'' (31 May 2010) Expert in [[Ukrainian politics]] Dr. [[Taras Kuzio]] believes that he has always prioritized personal revenge against Tymoshenko over Ukraine's national interests.{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/opinion/op_ed/detail/116989/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111116025514/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/opinion/op_ed/detail/116989/|archive-date=16 November 2011|title=Yushchenko's hand in the imprisonment of Yulia Tymoshenko|work=Kyiv Post|date=17 November 2011}} In her turn, Tymoshenko has blamed President Viktor Yushchenko for obstructing the government-proposed anti-crisis measures and efforts to form a broad coalition to battle the crisis. "The president is using flashy words today to deprive the nation, first of all its government, of the opportunity to counter the crisis, and to leave the nation without a government it logically needs" she said. "Viktor Yushchenko has no right to any criticism. He is the incumbent president. He only has the right to work and to serve Ukraine. He will have the right to criticize when he joins the opposition. Now he must work and answer for his moves".{{cite web|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/content/ukraine/tymoshenko-blames-yushchenko-for-obstructing-gover-43070.html|title=Tymoshenko blames Yushchenko for obstructing government's fight against crisis|publisher=Kyivpost.com|date=9 June 2009|access-date=28 December 2013}} Former Ukrainian Minister of Finance of Ukraine [[Viktor Pynzenyk]] has called Tymoshenko's decisions "normally guided by 'adventurous populism'", which she saw as a tool to "consolidate power in her own hands" and believed Tymoshenko should have "taken advantage of the opportunity presented by the [[2008–2009 Ukrainian financial crisis]] to reform".{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/91996/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101204135319/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/91996/|archive-date=4 December 2010|title=Pynzenyk, ex-finance minister, calls Tymoshenko "destructive force"|work=Kyiv Post|date=3 December 2010}} Party of Regions Deputy Head [[Borys Kolesnykov]] stated on 11 February 2010, "Tymoshenko was the most effective politician during the entire period of Ukraine's recent history".{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/59285/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100213164447/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/59285/|archive-date=13 February
