2010|title=Regions Party calls Tymoshenko most effective opposition figure in Ukraine|work=Kyiv Post|date=11 February 2010}} Former European High Representative for Common Foreign and Security Policy [[Javier Solana]] has called Tymoshenko "a patriot regardless of the position in which you have found yourself".{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/62960/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605065144/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/62960/|archive-date=5 June 2011|title=Solana happy with Tymoshenko's constructive mood in opposition|work=Kyiv Post|date=1 April 2010}} Yanukovych stated about Tymoshenko on 13 May 2010, "She likes to create a sensation. We have grown used to this extravagant woman".{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66603|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100515071109/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/66603/|archive-date=15 May 2010|title=Yanukovych says will not intervene over Tymoshenko|work=Kyiv Post|date=13 May 2010}} Vitaly Chepinoha has closely collaborated with Tymoshenko during [[elections in Ukraine|various elections]] for more than a decade.{{cite news|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/53251/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091123085548/https://www.kyivpost.com/news/politics/detail/53251|archive-date=23 November 2009|title=Paid advisers descend on candidates, nation|work=Kyiv Post|date=19 November 2009}} In some newspapers and television programs, Tymoshenko has been referred to as Lady Yu (Ледi Ю, Леди Ю).{{cite web |url=https://www.google.com.au/search?hl=en&expIds=25657,27642&sugexp=ldymls&xhr=t&q=%D0%9B%D0%B5%D0%B4%D0%B8+%D0%AE&cp=6&client=firefox-a&hs=udV&rls=org.mozilla:en-US:official&um=1&ie=UTF-8&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1105&bih=589&tbm=isch&gws_rd=cr,ssl&ei=OZChVr3UA4H60ASY9oTwAg |title=Images of Леди Ю |access-date=18 February 2011}}
{{cite web |url=https://www.google.com/images?um=1&hl=en&client=firefox-a&rls=org.mozilla%3Aen-US%3Aofficial&biw=1105&bih=589&tbs=isch%3A1&sa=1&q=%D0%9B%D0%B5%D0%B4i+%D0%AE&btnG=Search&aq=f&aqi=&aql=&oq=&gs_rfai=Images |title=of Ледi Ю (less extent) |date=3 October 2007 |access-date=18 February 2011}}
{{cite web |url=https://www.google.com/images?um=1&hl=en&client=firefox-a&rls=org.mozilla%3Aen-US%3Aofficial&biw=1105&bih=589&tbs=isch%3A1&sa=1&q=Lady+Yu&btnG=Search&aq=f&aqi=g2g-m2&aql=&oq=&gs_rfai= |title=Images of Lady Yu (lesser extent) |date=19 January 2011 |access-date=18 February 2011}}

{{in lang|uk}} [https://web.archive.org/web/20110727225023/https://5.ua/newsline/179//71350/ Історія об'єднання "Нашої України" та БЮТ [репортаж, відео]], [[5 Kanal (Ukraine)|5 Kanal]] (12 December 2010) ===Awards=== * Order of Saint Varvara, Great Martyr from [[Ukrainian Orthodox Church (Moscow Patriarchate)|Ukrainian Orthodox Church of Moscow Patriarchate]] (1998) * Third-most powerful woman in the world, rating by ''[[Forbes]]'' magazine (July 2005) * "Person of the year in Central and Eastern Europe" award at [[Economic Forum in Krynica|International Economic Forum]] in [[Krynica-Zdrój]], [[Poland]] (September 2005){{cite web|url= https://www.forum-ekonomiczne.pl/?lang=en |title= Forum Ekonomiczne |publisher= Forum-ekonomiczne.pl |date= 18 December 2013 |access-date= 28 December 2013}} * "[[Prix de la fondation Crans Montana]]" award for efficient governance and anti-corruption campaign at the annual session of the [[Crans Montana Forum]] (December 2005){{cite web |url= https://cmf.ch/prix |title= Prix de la Fondation | Crans Montana Forum – Committed to a more Humane and Impartial World |publisher= Cmf.ch |date= 12 November 2011 |access-date= 28 December 2013 |archive-date= 14 October 2013 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20131014204453/http://cmf.ch/prix |url-status= dead}}{{cite web |url= https://photo.ukrinform.ua/eng/current/photo.php?id=496414 |title= Yevheniya Tymoshenko receives award in Geneva |publisher= Photo.ukrinform.ua |access-date= 28 December 2013 |archive-date= 1 April 2022 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20220401040139/http://photo.ukrinform.ua/eng/current/photo.php?id=496414 |url-status= dead}}{{#tag:ref|Tymoshenko's daughter [[Yevhenia Tymoshenko]] received a medal for her contribution to the defense of democracy and human rights from the same organization in October 2012.|group=nb}} * "For Political Courage" by French magazine "[[Politique internationale]]"; it was the first award given to a Ukrainian nominee over the last 25 years of the magazine's history (March 2007) {{cite web|url= https://www.unian.ua/politics/39109-timoshenko-pojihala-do-frantsiji-za-nagorodoyu-politique-internationale.html|title= Tymoshenko went to France for the Politique Internationale award |publisher= unian |date= 27 March 2007 |access-date= 27 March 2007}} * Award by American NGO [[Conservative Political Action Conference]] for contribution to democracy development. (March 2007) * "Shakhtarska Slava" award on Coalminer Day in [[Luhansk]] (2008){{cite web|url= https://focus.ua/politics/25486|title= Tymoshenko was awarded the miner's order |publisher= focus|date= 29 August 2008 |access-date= 29 August 2008}} * [[Order of the Holy Sepulchre (Catholic)|The Order of the Holy Sepulchre]] award from the [[Greek Orthodox Church of Jerusalem|Jerusalem Orthodox Church]], given to Tymoshenko by [[Patriarch Theophilos III of Jerusalem]] (October 2008) * The Saint Andrew the First-Called Order of II degree (the highest church award of the [[Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the Kyivan Patriarchate]]) from Apostolic Patriarch of Ukraine [[Filaret (Denysenko)|Philaret]] (2011) * The Pope [[Boniface VIII]] International Award (October 2012). (The first person to receive the award was [[Pope John Paul II]].){{cite web|url= https://www.kyivpost.com/article/content/ukraine-politics/tymoshenko-decorated-with-pope-boniface-viii-award-311372.html|title=Tymoshenko decorated with Pope Boniface VIII award|work=KyivPost |date= 10 August 2012|accessdate= 10 August 2012}} * [[Manuel Carrasco Formiguera]] medal for her contribution to the defense of democracy and freedom and the struggle for the restoration of the rule of law in Ukraine, awarded by the [[Democratic Union of Catalonia]] party (July 2013). {{cite web |url= https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_11_07_2013_01%20 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20140223050204/https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/article/yulia_tymoshenko_11_07_2013_01 |archive-date= 23 February 2014 |title= Yulia Tymoshenko awarded for her contribution to defending democracy |publisher= Tymoshenko.ua |access-date= 28 December 2013 }} * On 18 October 2014, in [[Khmelnytskyi, Ukraine|Khmelnytskyi]], journalists presented Yulia Tymoshenko with the Yakov Halchevsky Prize "For significant contribution to the development of democracy and asceticism in state-building in Ukraine." The Batkivshchyna leader was awarded this prize in 2011. The diploma was presented to Yulia Tymoshenko in the Pechersk court, and the award was presented to Yakov Galchevsky's book "Against the Red Occupiers" by journalists in 2014.{{cite web|url=https://batkivshchyna.com.ua/news/20890.html|title=Журналисты Хмельницкой вручили Юлии Тимошенко награду за развитие государства|publisher=batkivshchyna|date=18 October 2014|url-status=dead|access-date=20 January 2015|archive-url=https://archive.today/20141021203511/http://batkivshchyna.com.ua/news/20890.html|archive-date=21 October 2014}} * In March 2023, Yulia Tymoshenko received the [[George H. W. Bush|Bush]]-[[Margaret Thatcher|Thatcher]] Award for Freedom and Democracy.{{Cite web |trans-title= Yulia Tymoshenko received the prestigious Bush-Thatcher Freedom and Democracy Award|script-title=uk: Юлія Тимошенко отримала визначну нагороду Буша-Тетчер за Свободу і Демократію|url= https://dv-gazeta.info/dneprnews/yuliya-timoshenko-otrimala-viznachnu-nagorodu-busha-tetcher-za-svobodu-i-demokratiyu.html|access-date=2023-03-31|website=[[dv-gazeta.info]] |language=uk}} ===Tymoshenko's positions in national ratings=== In 2004, ''[[Korrespondent]]'' magazine named Yulia Tymoshenko "Revolutionary of the Year".{{Cite web|url=https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-other_news/203153-korrespondent_magazine_names_personalities_of_2004_40703.html|title=KORRESPONDENT MAGAZINE NAMES PERSONALITIES OF 2004|website=www.ukrinform.net|access-date=18 April 2021|archive-date=18 April 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210418160158/https://www.ukrinform.net/rubric-other_news/203153-korrespondent_magazine_names_personalities_of_2004_40703.html|url-status=dead}} In 2006 ''Korrespondent'' magazine ranked Tymoshenko as its 2005 "Personality of the Year", naming her in the TOP 100 most influential politicians of Ukraine, 2nd place (Women with nimbus), Person of the year.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyivpost.com/article/content/ukraine-politics/korrespondent-magazine-names-tymoshenko-personalit-23711.html|title=Korrespondent magazine names Tymoshenko Personality of the Year – Jan. 19, 2006|date=18 January 2006|website=KyivPost}}{{request quotation|date=May 2019}} 2007 Yulia Tymoshenko, ''Focus'' magazine, the most influential women of Ukraine, 1st place. Yulia Tymoshenko, Korrespondent magazine, TOP 100 the most influential politics of Ukraine, 4th place (Woman-brand), Person of the year. Yulia Tymoshenko, Focus magazine, 200 the most influential Ukrainians, 2nd place. 2009 Yulia Tymoshenko, ''Korrespondent'' magazine, TOP 100 the most influential Ukrainians, 1st place (Dream women). Yulia Tymoshenko, Focus magazine, the most influential women of Ukraine, 1st place. Yulia Tymoshenko, Focus magazine, TOP 200 the most influential politicians of Ukraine, 1st place. Between 2007 and 2013 in Ukraine, Tymoshenko was the most popular politician on the Internet,{{cite web |url=https://delo.ua/ukraine/rejting-populjarnosti-politikov-v-internete-timoshenko-obognala-ja-193235/ |title=(Russian language) Popularity rating of politicians on the Internet: Tymoshenko ahead of Yanukovych |publisher=Delo.ua |date=26 December 2012 |access-date=28 December 2013 |archive-date=30 December 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131230235622/http://delo.ua/ukraine/rejting-populjarnosti-politikov-v-internete-timoshenko-obognala-ja-193235/ |url-status=dead}} in blogs{{cite web|first=Максим |last=Кутик |url=https://ain.ua/2012/02/09/72994 |title=(Russian language) Yulia Tymoshenko is the most popular politician in the blogs of Ukraine. 2007 |date=9 February 2012 |publisher=Ain.ua |access-date=28 December 2013}}{{cite web|first=Максим |last=Кутик |url=https://ain.ua/2012/02/09/72994 |title=Yulia Tymoshenko is the most popular politician in the blogs of Ukraine |publisher=Ain.ua |date=9 February 2012 |access-date=28 December 2013}} and social networks.{{cite web|url=https://uainfo.org/yandex/207805-samyy-populyarnyy-politik-v-odnoklassnikah-i-vkontakte-eto-timoshenko.html |script-title=ru:Самый популярный политик в «Одноклассниках» и «ВКонтакте» – это Тимошенко |trans-title=The most popular politician in "Odnoklassniki" and "VKontakte" – is Tymoshenko |language=ru|work=UAINFO |date=15 October 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131017034733/https://uainfo.org/yandex/207805-samyy-populyarnyy-politik-v-odnoklassnikah-i-vkontakte-eto-timoshenko.html |archive-date=17 October 2013}} {{As of | 2010}}, Yulia Tymoshenko was the most popular foreign politician in the Russian media. {{cite web|url= https://top.novostimira.biz/article_13971.html |title= (Russian language) Yulia Tymoshenko is the most popular foreign policy in the Russian media |publisher= Top.novostimira.biz |date= 1 October 2010 |access-date= 28 December 2013 }} In 2012 the national rating (28 December 2012) by the [[Razumkov Center]] and the "{{ill|Foundation for Democratic Initiatives|uk|Демократичні ініціативи|vertical-align=sup}}" recognised Yulia Tymoshenko as the best prime minister of Ukraine – 19.5%, compared with Viktor Yanukovych – 11.4%, Mykola Azarov – 8.6%, Leonid Kuchma – 5.6%, Viktor Yushchenko – 3.9%, Pavel Lazarenko – 2.2%. {{cite news |url= https://korrespondent.net/ukraine/politics/1446504-timoshenko-lidiruet-v-rejtinge-premer-ministrov-ukrainy-opros |script-title=ru:Тимошенко лидирует в рейтинге премьер-министров Украины – опрос |trans-title=Yulia Tymoshenko leads in the ratings as the best Prime Minister of Ukraine – survey |language=ru|work=Korrespondent.net |date=27 December 2012|access-date=29 January 2016 }} According to the sociological group "Rating" Yulia Tymoshenko was ranked as third most trusted politician in early April 2021, behind President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and then-Chairman of the Verkhovna Rada [[Dmytro Razumkov]].{{Cite web|title= Рейтинг доверия среди украинцев возглавили Зеленский, Разумков и Тимошенко|url=https://www.rbc.ua/rus/news/reyting-doveriya-sredi-ukraintsev-vozglavili-1617963415.html|website=[[rbc.ua]]|access-date=9 April 2021}} === Documentaries about Tymoshenko === * 2009 — «Julia» – American film studio «Coppola Productions».{{Cite web|url=https://www.imdb.com/title/tt1922793/|title=Yulia|via=IMDb}} * 2011 — documentary (25 minutes) shown to delegates to the NATO Parliamentary Assembly, 8 October 2011.{{Cite web|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oQlt4W2PJ1I|title=The trial of Yulia Tymoshenko. True story.|date=7 October 2011 |via=www.youtube.com}} === Books about Tymoshenko === * 2007 — «Julia, Yulechka» – Antonina Nikolaevna Ulyakhina (Dnipropetrovsk) * 2007 — «Julia, Julia Vladimirovna» Antonina Nikolaevna Ulyakhina (Dnipropetrovsk) ==Cultural references== * "Yulia Tymoshenko", a play by [[Adriana Altaras]] and [[Maksim Kurochkin]] in the [[Hans Otto Theater]] in [[Potsdam]], Germany. (September 2006){{cite web |url= https://www.potsdam.de/cms/ziel/34310/en/ |title= The Hans Otto Theater |publisher= Potsdam.de |access-date= 28 December 2013 |url-status= dead |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20131230232625/https://www.potsdam.de/cms/ziel/34310/en/ |archive-date= 30 December 2013 |df= dmy-all}} * The play "Who Wants to Kill Yulia Tymoshenko?", first performed at the opening night of the 2013 [[Edinburgh Festival Fringe]], portrayed the political fight of Yulia Tymoshenko and her imprisonment. (August 2013){{cite web |url= https://www.whatsonstage.com/edinburgh-theatre/reviews/08-2013/who-wants-to-kill-yulia-tymoshenko-edinburgh-fring_31564.html%20 |title= Who Wants to Kill Yulia Tymoshenko? (Edinburgh Fringe) – Reviews |publisher= Whatsonstage.com |date= 8 August 2013 |access-date= 28 December 2013 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20140305210417/https://www.whatsonstage.com/edinburgh-theatre/reviews/08-2013/who-wants-to-kill-yulia-tymoshenko-edinburgh-fring_31564.html |archive-date= 5 March 2014 |url-status= dead |df= dmy-all}} * On 4 October 2014 in Milan, Italy, Yulia Tymoshenko's daughter [[Eugenia Tymoshenko]] presented the book "Ukraine, Gas and Handcuffs: The Trial of Yulia Tymoshenko" (Italian: «Ucraina, gas e manette: il processo a Yulia Tymoshenko»[https://books.google.com/books?id=aL_EoQEACAAJ ''Ucraina, gas e manette: il processo a Yulia Tymoshenko''], by Matteo Cazzulani, {{ISBN|9788891157478}}. ). The title, "Ukraine, Gas and Handcuffs: The Trial of Yulia Tymoshenko", demonstrates a clear understanding that energy is the key source of Ukraine's dependence. The author, Matteo Cazzulani, draws a clear parallel between Ukraine and the fate of Yulia Tymoshenko, denied her freedom because of her fight against corruption, the oligarchy and the dependence of the Ukrainian energy-sector on Russian energy.{{cite web |url= https://milano.corriere.it/notizie/cronaca/14_ottobre_05/figlia-yulia-tymoshenko-diritti-umani-negati-mia-madre-0f965f8c-4c87-11e4-8c5c-557ef01adf3d.shtml|title= La figlia di Yulia Tymoshenko "Diritti umani negati a mia madre"|publisher= milano.corriere.it|date= 5 October 2014|access-date= 5 October 2014}}{{cite web|url= https://www.lintraprendente.it/2014/10/vera-storia-del-processo-tymoshenko-pilotato-da-mosca/|title= Vera storia del processo Tymoshenko. Pilotato da Mosca|date= 5 October 2014|access-date= 5 October 2014}}{{cite web|url= https://www.tymoshenko.ua/en/news-en/book-on-persecution-of-yulia-tymoshenko-presented-in-italy/|title= Book on
